examples.title: 1900: the fairground of the century
examples.subtitle: Paris opens the new century as a total designed spectacle: Art Nouveau entrances, electric pavilions, moving sidewalks, posters, gowns, glass, and colonial display all compete to define modernity.
examples.feeling: the new century entering through an iron flower
primaryLens: the Paris Exposition makes Art Nouveau, electricity, and spectacle public
Guimard's Metro entrances turn infrastructure into botanical metalwork
poster culture and color lithography make the street a designed surface
craft revival and industrial display argue over what modern luxury should be
cinema, dance, and electric light teach design to think in motion

recipes.name: Metro flower
recipes.useFor: transit tools, civic wayfinding, cultural districts, event entrances.
recipes.palette: dark iron, warm glass, muted green, cream, amber.
recipes.type: custom Art Nouveau lettering integrated into structure.
recipes.layout: threshold-first, symmetrical portal, curved uprights, sign as canopy.
recipes.imagery: stems, seed pods, lamps, glass awnings, station names.
recipes.motion: arrival through an arch, lamp glow, sliding underground descent.
recipes.risk: generic vine decoration without infrastructure logic.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: make the ornament carry a practical function.

recipes.name: Exposition promenade
recipes.useFor: launches, fairs, museums, product ecosystems, immersive sites.
recipes.palette: cream stone, gilded bronze, night blue, electric white, poster red.
recipes.type: official serif hierarchy mixed with hand-lettered display.
recipes.layout: map, pavilion, ticket, catalogue, and route as one system.
recipes.imagery: domes, bridges, lamps, crowds, machines, flags, souvenirs.
recipes.motion: procession, reveal, crowd drift, illuminated night sequence.
recipes.risk: reducing the year to decorative swirls.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: include logistics, maps, tickets, and crowd movement.

recipes.name: Lithographic street
recipes.useFor: posters, editorial campaigns, theater, food, drink, fashion.
recipes.palette: flat red, ochre, black, sage, cream, tobacco brown.
recipes.type: drawn display letters shaped by the image.
recipes.layout: single dominant figure, integrated product name, ornamental frame.
recipes.imagery: performers, hats, smoke, bicycles, bottles, floral curves.
recipes.motion: poster seen while walking; bold silhouette first, details second.
recipes.risk: sterile vector Art Nouveau with no print grain.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: lithographic texture and imperfect color registration.

recipes.name: Opalescent object
recipes.useFor: jewelry, beauty, fragrance, collectibles, premium packaging.
recipes.palette: pearl, peacock, horn brown, enamel red, gold, moss green.
recipes.type: restrained serif or small drawn label.
recipes.layout: object centered like a specimen, asymmetry in detail.
recipes.imagery: dragonflies, orchids, women, water, glass, insects.
recipes.motion: slow turn, changing translucency, glint under warm light.
recipes.risk: fantasy fairy imagery with no material intelligence.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: horn, enamel, glass, patina, and craft joinery.

promptSeeds:
Design this through a 1900 lens: the Paris Exposition has turned electricity,
posters, pavilions, and Guimard's Metro entrances into a public design system.
Keep Art Nouveau connected to infrastructure rather than using vines as decoration.

Give me three 1900-informed directions:
1. Metro flower
2. Exposition promenade
3. Lithographic street
For each, explain the historical lineage, typography, color, material, motion, and what to avoid.

Critique this brand as if it appeared in Paris in 1900. Does it understand the
relationship between poster, pavilion, electric light, transit entrance, and craft object?
What details prove the year?

objects: Hector Guimard's Paris Metro entrance components
Rene Lalique Art Nouveau jewelry shown at the Paris Exposition
Emile Galle cameo glass
Tiffany favrile glass and lamps
Kodak Brownie camera
Exposition medals, tickets, guidebooks, and souvenirs
printAndGraphics: Paris Exposition Universelle posters, maps, catalogues, and official programs
Jules Cheret posters for Parisian entertainment and products
Alphonse Mucha posters and decorative panels circulating at the turn of the century
Art Nouveau lettering and signboards for Metro stations
Sheet-music covers for cafe-concert, music hall, and ragtime
spaces: The Paris Exposition Universelle grounds
Guimard's Paris Metro entrances
The Grand Palais and Petit Palais
Pont Alexandre III
Art Nouveau interiors by Guimard and contemporaries
Paris music halls and Loie Fuller performance environments

antiCliches: Generic steampunk gears
A flat wallpaper of random vines
Victorian clutter with no modern infrastructure
Pure Beaux-Arts pomp without posters, transit, and electricity
A fairy illustration detached from real materials
Clean digital curves with no lithographic or metalworking texture
Later Art Deco geometry
A fantasy Paris with no crowds, tickets, maps, or machines

examples.title: 1901: ornament learns discipline
examples.subtitle: The Belle Epoque remains lush, but Glasgow, Darmstadt, Vienna, Buffalo, and Craftsman reform pull the curve toward structure, geometry, rooms, and systems.
examples.feeling: the floral line being squared into a room
primaryLens: Secession and Glasgow design make ornament flatter, squarer, and more architectural
Darmstadt tests the artists' colony as a total designed environment
The Craftsman turns American reform taste toward honest furniture and domestic moral clarity
the Pan-American Exposition stages electric spectacle and imperial display in Buffalo
radio, aviation experiments, and the motor age make invisible systems feel near

recipes.name: Glasgow severity
recipes.useFor: boutique culture, literary brands, interiors, invitations, editorial systems.
recipes.palette: pale grey, white, black, muted rose, olive, soft gold.
recipes.type: tall narrow lettering, restrained serif, panel-set captions.
recipes.layout: vertical proportions, high margins, square panels, elongated figures.
recipes.imagery: stylized roses, ladders, high-backed chairs, symbolic women.
recipes.motion: slow vertical reveal, panel transitions, restrained fade.
recipes.risk: making it generic fairy Art Nouveau.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: severity, whiteness, and rectilinear structure.

recipes.name: Craftsman ethic
recipes.useFor: furniture, home goods, sustainability, publishing, workshops.
recipes.palette: oak brown, leather, moss, cream paper, iron black.
recipes.type: sturdy serif, practical headings, magazine-like hierarchy.
recipes.layout: honest margins, object diagrams, room plans, editorial calm.
recipes.imagery: joinery, hearth, tools, chairs, bungalows, woven textiles.
recipes.motion: hand assembly, page turn, cabinet door, firelight.
recipes.risk: confusing Arts and Crafts with rustic farmhouse nostalgia.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: visible construction and moral clarity.

recipes.name: Electric exposition
recipes.useFor: events, civic festivals, museums, nighttime experiences.
recipes.palette: electric white, patriotic red, lagoon blue, cream, gold.
recipes.type: ornamental display paired with official serif information.
recipes.layout: axial fairground plan, tower emblem, ticket and guidebook system.
recipes.imagery: towers, domes, flags, bulbs, lagoons, crowds.
recipes.motion: illumination sequence, crowd promenade, searchlight sweep.
recipes.risk: triumphalist spectacle without acknowledging instability.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: maps, guidebooks, and temporary architecture.

recipes.name: Secession page
recipes.useFor: art publications, galleries, cultural essays, refined packaging.
recipes.palette: black, cream, dull gold, moss green, brick red.
recipes.type: geometric lettering, disciplined capitals, serif text blocks.
recipes.layout: frame, panel, square, border, emblem, generous margin.
recipes.imagery: flat flowers, masks, symbolic figures, repeated motifs.
recipes.motion: page sliding inside a frame, ornament appearing by rule.
recipes.risk: too much free-flowing vine; this recipe needs discipline.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: panel logic and printed restraint.

promptSeeds:
Design this through a 1901 lens: Darmstadt is testing the artists' colony,
Glasgow is disciplining the Art Nouveau line, and The Craftsman is turning
American reform furniture into an editorial program. Keep spectacle and reform distinct.

Give me three 1901-informed directions:
1. Glasgow severity
2. Craftsman ethic
3. Electric exposition
For each, explain the historical lineage, typography, color, material, motion, and what to avoid.

Critique this interior as if it were published in 1901. Is it reform design,
Secession discipline, Glasgow symbolism, or exposition spectacle? What evidence supports that lineage?

objects: Gustav Stickley Craftsman furniture
Glasgow School high-backed chairs and decorative panels
Darmstadt Artists' Colony furniture, ceramics, and metalwork
Pan-American Exposition souvenirs, tickets, and electric-lamp imagery
Typewriters, gramophones, cameras, and bicycles in turn-of-century domestic life
printAndGraphics: The Craftsman magazine, first issued in 1901
Pan-American Exposition posters, maps, programmes, and guidebooks
Vienna Secession and Glasgow-style graphic panels
Music-hall and ragtime sheet-music covers
Design-journal reproductions of interiors and furniture
spaces: Mathildenhöhe, Darmstadt Artists' Colony
Joseph Maria Olbrich's Ernst Ludwig House
Buffalo's Pan-American Exposition grounds
Glasgow School and Mackintosh-related interiors
American Arts and Crafts domestic interiors

antiCliches: 1900 Paris repeated without reform
Random Mucha hair on every surface
Rustic modern farmhouse
Pure Victorian heaviness with no new discipline
Later Bauhaus minimalism
Deco zigzags or 1920s geometry
Electric spectacle without printed systems
Glasgow roses without vertical severity

examples.title: 1902: the modern decorative arts declare themselves
examples.subtitle: Turin gives Art Nouveau an international decorative-arts platform, Vienna stages Klimt's Beethoven Frieze, Photo-Secession forms in New York, and Mackintosh begins the Hill House.
examples.feeling: the decorative arts standing in public and naming their ambitions
primaryLens: Turin's decorative arts exhibition makes the new style international and comparative
Vienna Secession turns exhibition design into a ritualized total environment
Mackintosh's Hill House begins translating Glasgow severity into domestic architecture
Photo-Secession reframes photography as an art and graphic culture
early film fantasy and illustrated print sharpen design's sense of sequence

recipes.name: Beethoven room
recipes.useFor: exhibitions, museums, music brands, cultural installations.
recipes.palette: ivory, black, dull gold, chalk grey, muted green.
recipes.type: Secession capitals, framed labels, ceremonial serif text.
recipes.layout: procession, wall frieze, central object, flanking panels.
recipes.imagery: allegory, figures, gold fields, sculpture, music references.
recipes.motion: slow gallery walk, sequential reveal, pause before a central shrine.
recipes.risk: Klimt pastiche with no exhibition sequence.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: route, wall label, sculpture, and music as one environment.

recipes.name: Turin decorative arts
recipes.useFor: furniture systems, craft marketplaces, design fairs, catalogs.
recipes.palette: warm paper, chestnut, olive, brick red, dull brass.
recipes.type: refined serif with national-style display accents.
recipes.layout: catalogue plates, object groupings, room vignettes, comparative sections.
recipes.imagery: chairs, glass, lamps, ceramics, textiles, national pavilions.
recipes.motion: catalogue page turn, display-case reveal, fair route.
recipes.risk: blending all Art Nouveau into one generic swirl.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: compare regional styles instead of flattening them.

recipes.name: Pictorial photograph
recipes.useFor: photography portfolios, archives, artist identities, memory products.
recipes.palette: platinum grey, sepia, cream, charcoal, soft black.
recipes.type: quiet serif, small captions, generous margins.
recipes.layout: mounted print, plate mark, title page, gallery sequence.
recipes.imagery: atmospheric portraits, city haze, soft focus, hand-pulled prints.
recipes.motion: fade in from paper grain, focus breathing, gallery light.
recipes.risk: Instagram sepia nostalgia.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: print process, mount, caption, and exhibition context.

recipes.name: Moon-stage fantasy
recipes.useFor: film titles, theater, games, science education, speculative brands.
recipes.palette: night blue, smoke grey, moon cream, stage red, brass.
recipes.type: theatrical serif, poster lettering, hand-drawn titles.
recipes.layout: proscenium frame, central emblem, sequential scenes.
recipes.imagery: moon face, telescopes, rockets, costumes, painted flats.
recipes.motion: jump cut, puff of smoke, stage transformation, tableau.
recipes.risk: sleek space-age futurism.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: visible theatrical artifice and hand-painted sets.

promptSeeds:
Design this through a 1902 lens: Turin has gathered modern decorative arts
internationally, Vienna's Beethoven Exhibition has made gallery sequence ceremonial,
and Photo-Secession has begun treating photographs as authored prints.

Give me three 1902-informed directions:
1. Beethoven room
2. Turin decorative arts
3. Pictorial photograph
For each, explain the historical lineage, typography, color, material, motion, and what to avoid.

Critique this exhibition identity as if it were made in 1902. Does it behave like
a catalogue, a Secession room, a Glasgow house, or a cinematic fantasy? What evidence proves the year?

objects: Objects displayed at the Turin International Exhibition of Modern Decorative Art
Max Klinger's Beethoven sculpture in the Vienna Secession exhibition
Furniture and fittings for Mackintosh's Hill House commission
Pictorial photographs by Alfred Stieglitz and Edward Steichen
Beatrix Potter's small-format The Tale of Peter Rabbit
printAndGraphics: Turin 1902 exhibition catalogues, posters, and plates

Klimt's Beethoven Frieze reproductions and studies
Photo-Secession exhibition material
Posters and still imagery for Melies's A Trip to the Moon
spaces: The Turin International Exhibition of Modern Decorative Art
The Vienna Secession Beethoven Exhibition installation
The Hill House, Helensburgh, as begun in 1902 and completed later
Photo-Secession exhibition contexts in New York
Early cinema theaters and fairground projection spaces

antiCliches: A single generic Art Nouveau wallpaper
Paris 1900 repeated without Turin, Vienna, or photography
Later Art Deco luxury
Bauhaus minimalism
Space-age rockets for A Trip to the Moon
Klimt gold used without Secession exhibition structure
Soft-focus photography with no print or mount logic
Craftsman rusticity applied to every country

examples.title: 1903: systems take off
examples.subtitle: Wiener Werkstätte is founded, Camera Work begins, Ford Motor Company appears, and powered flight turns modern design toward workshops, media, mobility, and the engineered body.
examples.feeling: the handmade ideal meeting the engine and the printed plate
primaryLens: Wiener Werkstätte makes the workshop into a coordinated modern brand
Camera Work gives photography a luxurious printed voice
Ford Motor Company and the Wright flight pull design toward mobility systems
Arts and Crafts reform keeps the handmade object morally central
early cinema and popular music make sequence, promotion, and reproducibility unavoidable

recipes.name: Wiener workshop
recipes.useFor: boutiques, studios, maker platforms, cultural retail, craft brands.
recipes.palette: black, white, silver, warm wood, dull brass, muted green.
recipes.type: geometric capitals, monogram, stamped maker's mark, restrained serif.
recipes.layout: square grid, product plate, label system, catalogue spread.
recipes.imagery: boxes, baskets, textiles, metalwork, workshops, marks.
recipes.motion: stamp, fold, open drawer, object placed on gridded surface.
recipes.risk: later Bauhaus minimalism or luxury branding without labor.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: maker attribution and coordinated object families.

recipes.name: Camera Work plate
recipes.useFor: photography portfolios, archives, artist publications, cultural journals.
recipes.palette: warm black, platinum grey, cream, sepia, soft brown.
recipes.type: quiet serif, small capitals, spacious captions.
recipes.layout: generous margins, single plate, tissue-guard feeling, edition rhythm.
recipes.imagery: atmospheric portraits, city haze, photogravure texture, mounted prints.
recipes.motion: page turn, paper lift, slow image emergence.
recipes.risk: generic sepia filter.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: paper, plate, margin, caption, and publication sequence.

recipes.name: First flight engineering
recipes.useFor: mobility products, aerospace concepts, technical education, prototypes.
recipes.palette: canvas cream, spruce wood, wire grey, oil black, sky blue.
recipes.type: technical serif, patent labels, workshop notes, stamped numerals.
recipes.layout: diagram, elevation, parts list, field test log.
recipes.imagery: struts, propellers, wings, sand, bicycle mechanics, tools.
recipes.motion: vibration, lift, glide, fragile takeoff, wind pressure.
recipes.risk: sleek jet-age futurism.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: fragility, exposed structure, and bicycle-shop engineering.

recipes.name: Train-robbery cinema
recipes.useFor: film brands, narrative tools, games, entertainment campaigns.
recipes.palette: dusty brown, black, cream, railway red, smoke grey.
recipes.type: theater poster lettering, serif titles, handbills.
recipes.layout: scene sequence, chase path, poster tableau, ticket strip.
recipes.imagery: trains, bandits, telegraph poles, smoke, pistols, spectators.
recipes.motion: cut, chase, stop, direct address, flicker.
recipes.risk: later Western nostalgia with no early-cinema apparatus.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: projection, handbill, and theatrical staging cues.

promptSeeds:
Design this through a 1903 lens: Wiener Werkstätte has just been founded,
Camera Work has begun publishing, Ford Motor Company is new, and the Wright Flyer
has made powered flight real. Keep workshop craft and fragile engineering in tension.

Give me three 1903-informed directions:
1. Wiener workshop
2. Camera Work plate
3. First flight engineering
For each, explain the historical lineage, typography, color, material, motion, and what to avoid.

Critique this product system as if it appeared in 1903. Does it behave like a
workshop catalogue, an art-photography journal, a motor-age prototype, or early cinema publicity?
What evidence supports that lineage?

antiCliches: Fully developed Bauhaus identity
Streamlined aircraft or chrome automobile futurism
Generic sepia photography without publication craft
Victorian clutter unrelated to workshop systems
Random Art Nouveau vines with no maker's mark or object family
Air travel glamour; flight is fragile and experimental
Mass-production Fordism from the Model T era
Modern cinema without flicker, handbills, and theatrical roots

examples.title: 1904: the square, the subway, and the prairie
examples.subtitle: Vienna's workshops harden into geometric luxury, New York opens its subway, Mackintosh completes the Hill House, and Wright designs the Larkin Building as a new office system.
examples.feeling: ornament crossing a tiled platform into modern systems
primaryLens: Vienna 1900 moves from symbolic ornament toward coordinated geometric total design
New York's subway makes metropolitan movement a tiled, signed, infrastructural experience
Mackintosh's Hill House completes a severe domestic total artwork
Wright's Larkin Building design and prairie houses push American architecture toward integrated modern space
motor culture, photography, and illustrated media keep daily life accelerating

recipes.layout: platform rhythm, repeated plaques, route strip, entrance threshold.

sectionMap.label: Design climate
sectionMap.description: 1904 is pulled between geometric total design and metropolitan infrastructure.

sectionMap.label: Timeline signals
sectionMap.description: The New York City subway opens, Mackintosh's Hill House is completed, Frank Lloyd Wright de...

sectionMap.label: Typography
sectionMap.description: 1904 typography is becoming civic, tiled, gridded, and still ceremonial.

sectionMap.label: Graphic design
sectionMap.description: Graphic design in 1904 has to direct, certify, and commemorate.

sectionMap.label: Product design
sectionMap.description: 1904 product design is increasingly tied to systems of use.

sectionMap.label: Architecture
sectionMap.description: Architecture and interiors are the center of 1904.

sectionMap.label: Fashion
sectionMap.description: Fashion in 1904 is still structured, but the modern person is increasingly mobile.

sectionMap.label: Music
sectionMap.description: 1904 music culture is theatrical, recorded, and internationally styled.

sectionMap.label: Film
sectionMap.description: Film in 1904 continues to grow from attraction into repeatable entertainment.

sectionMap.label: Surface
sectionMap.description: 1904 color is tiled, gridded, earthy, and atmospheric.

sectionMap.label: Anti-cliches
sectionMap.description: Do not make 1904 look like:

examples.title: 1905: wild color, disciplined line
examples.subtitle: Fauves explode color in Paris, Die Brücke forms in Dresden, and Vienna's total-design dream begins to harden into stone, mosaic, furniture, and brandable surface.
examples.feeling: elegance under pressure from wild color and hard edges
primaryLens: fauvist color breaks naturalism into emotional signal
die brucke turns youth, woodcut force, and urban anxiety into expressionist design pressure
viennese total design moves from workshops into monumental domestic architecture
the modern poster simplifies advertising into one memorable figure
nickelodeons make moving pictures a designed mass habit

recipes.name: Fauvist poster shock
recipes.useFor: campaigns, music, exhibitions, editorial covers, product launches.
recipes.palette: vermilion, emerald, ultramarine, acid yellow, black.
recipes.type: hand-lettered display, irregular capitals, strong poster hierarchy.
recipes.layout: one huge color field, one figure, compressed background, minimal copy.
recipes.imagery: faces, animals, dancers, trees, interiors rendered in emotional color.
recipes.motion: abrupt color cuts, fast wipes, painted flicker.
recipes.risk: looking like generic psychedelic color rather than 1905 Paris.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: flat lithographic color and a real Salon d'Automne reference.

recipes.name: Dresden woodcut youth
recipes.useFor: art spaces, zines, cultural festivals, manifestos, independent brands.
recipes.palette: black ink, raw cream paper, brick red, sap green.
recipes.type: carved, uneven, compact lettering that feels printed by hand.
recipes.layout: tight frame, rough border, figure pressed against the page.
recipes.imagery: bathers, streets, dancers, studio interiors, angular bodies.
recipes.motion: choppy cuts, carved-edge transitions, pressure marks.
recipes.risk: confusing Expressionism with later punk without the woodcut logic.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: visible block-print grain and Die Brücke's Dresden origin.

recipes.name: Wiener total room
recipes.useFor: interiors, luxury goods, hospitality, cultural identity, packaging.
recipes.palette: black, white, gold, olive, soft grey.
recipes.type: refined serif or Secession capitals, centered or gridded.
recipes.layout: rectangular panels, borders, repeated motifs, strict alignment.
recipes.imagery: squares, stylized plants, metalwork, mosaic, controlled ornament.
recipes.motion: measured panel reveals, hinge-like rotations, slow gilded glints.
recipes.risk: making Vienna look like generic Art Deco twenty years too early.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: Hoffmann geometry and Wiener Werkstätte craft references.

recipes.name: Nickelodeon street
recipes.useFor: event identities, video platforms, archives, playful civic projects.
recipes.palette: lamp black, paper white, ticket red, brass, smoky blue.
recipes.type: bold facade lettering, program serif, simple title-card hierarchy.
recipes.layout: storefront sign, ticket window, stacked bills, sequential frames.
recipes.imagery: projector cone, pianist, crowd, marquee bulbs, still photographs.
recipes.motion: flicker, iris, title card, quick scene changes.
recipes.risk: importing 1920s movie-palace glamour into a smaller 1905 venue.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: five-cent simplicity and short-program rhythm.

promptSeeds:
Design this through a 1905 lens: Fauvism has just erupted at the Salon d'Automne,
Die Brücke has formed in Dresden, and Hoffmann's Palais Stoclet is beginning in
Brussels. Keep the result between wild color, cut expressionist line, and
Viennese total-design discipline.

Give me three historically grounded 1905 directions:
1. Fauvist poster shock
2. Dresden woodcut youth
3. Wiener total room
For each, specify typography, color, material, layout, and the cliches to avoid.

Critique this brand as if it belonged to 1905. Does it understand Cappiello's
poster simplification, Die Brücke's woodcut force, or the Wiener Werkstätte's
coordinated interior logic?

objects: Gillette safety razor and replaceable blade system
Wiener Werkstätte silver, textiles, and furniture by Josef Hoffmann and Koloman Moser
Thonet bentwood chairs used in cafes and public interiors
Early gramophones, cameras, and catalogued domestic mechanisms
Hand-pulled Die Brücke woodcuts and printed announcements
printAndGraphics: Fauvist paintings shown at the 1905 Salon d'Automne
Leonetto Cappiello advertising posters in Paris
Vienna Secession and Wiener Werkstätte printed material
Die Brücke early woodcuts and group graphics
Nickelodeon posters, programs, and storefront signs
spaces: Palais Stoclet, Brussels, begun 1905 by Josef Hoffmann
Paris Salon d'Automne galleries in 1905
Dresden studios associated with Die Brücke
Pittsburgh's Nickelodeon theater opened by Harry Davis and John P. Harris
Vienna Secession and Wiener Werkstätte interiors

antiCliches: A generic Mucha poster with no Fauvist or Expressionist pressure
Later Art Deco geometry with chrome, skyscrapers, and jazz-age polish
Psychedelic color detached from Salon d'Automne painting
Steampunk brass machinery standing in for Belle Epoque technology
Viennese design reduced to Klimt gold without Hoffmann's strict geometry
Cinema shown as a grand movie palace instead of a small nickelodeon
Arts and Crafts nostalgia with no advertising, city, or mass-entertainment edge

sectionMap.label: Year thesis
sectionMap.description: elegance under pressure from wild color and hard edges

sectionMap.label: 1905 to 1904
sectionMap.description: Year-to-year change.

sectionMap.label: Design climate
sectionMap.description: 1905 is pulled between cultivated total design and expressive rupture.

sectionMap.label: Timeline signals
sectionMap.description: The 1905 Salon d'Automne introduces Fauvism to scandalized Paris, Louis Vauxcelles calls th...

sectionMap.label: Typography
sectionMap.description: 1905 typography is still ornamental, but its manners are weakening.

sectionMap.label: Graphic design
sectionMap.description: 1905 graphic design sits between the boulevard poster and the hand-pulled print.

sectionMap.label: Product design
sectionMap.description: 1905 products are still dressed in craft, but they are beginning to behave like systems.

sectionMap.label: Architecture
sectionMap.description: Architecture in 1905 is where ornament starts to become a complete operating system.

sectionMap.label: Fashion
sectionMap.description: Fashion in 1905 still loves the S-curve silhouette, elaborate hats, lace, embroidery, and t...

sectionMap.label: Music
sectionMap.description: 1905 music offers designers atmosphere rather than structure alone.

sectionMap.label: Film
sectionMap.description: The nickelodeon is the important design event of 1905.

sectionMap.label: Surface
sectionMap.description: 1905 color splits between two kinds of intensity.

sectionMap.label: Anti-cliches
sectionMap.description: Do not make 1905 look like:

examples.title: 1906: the curve becomes masonry
examples.subtitle: Gaudi begins Casa Mila, Vienna's disciplined reform spreads through furniture and interiors, and early cinema stretches from fairground novelty toward narrative spectacle.
examples.feeling: ornament learning to stand up as structure
primaryLens: catalan modernisme turns organic line into urban stone
secession discipline keeps simplifying ornament into geometry and total interiors
arts and crafts ethics remain active but face commercial modernity
early cinema expands narrative ambition and public appetite
fashion and poster culture make the body a designed silhouette

recipes.name: Casa Mila organism
recipes.useFor: architecture brands, cultural campaigns, hospitality, environmental graphics.
recipes.palette: limestone, iron black, ceramic blue, moss green, warm shadow.
recipes.type: restrained serif or hand-drawn capitals kept secondary to form.
recipes.layout: flowing facade rhythm, stacked openings, balcony-like interruptions.
recipes.imagery: stone waves, wrought iron, chimneys, sea forms, roof silhouettes.
recipes.motion: slow undulation, parallax stone, iron tendrils opening like balconies.
recipes.risk: turning Gaudi into generic fantasy architecture.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: urban apartment-house logic, not fairy-tale castle styling.

recipes.name: Secession room discipline
recipes.useFor: interiors, museums, premium packaging, editorial systems, stationery.
recipes.palette: cream, black, muted gold, olive, dusty rose.
recipes.type: spaced capitals, quiet serif, rectilinear letter spacing.
recipes.layout: panels, square motifs, borders, aligned captions, controlled margins.
recipes.imagery: stylized leaves, grids, chair backs, metal fixtures, textile repeats.
recipes.motion: measured reveals, panel slides, exact hinge movements.
recipes.risk: jumping forward to 1920s Deco luxury.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: Wiener Werkstätte craft and Hoffmann's severe geometry.

recipes.name: Poster attention machine
recipes.useFor: consumer brands, events, beverages, theater, transit advertising.
recipes.palette: saturated red, cream, black, bottle green, poster yellow.
recipes.type: large hand-lettered brand name, minimal supporting copy.
recipes.layout: one figure, one product, one field, strong distance readability.
recipes.imagery: mascot, performer, bottle, animal, oversized gesture.
recipes.motion: snap zoom to emblem, pasted-bill layering, tram-speed read.
recipes.risk: over-illustrating when the period's innovation is simplification.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: color-lithograph flatness and Cappiello-like economy.

recipes.name: Early cinema program
recipes.useFor: film archives, streaming retrospectives, event series, education.
recipes.palette: black, cream, ticket red, smoky grey, brass.
recipes.type: title-card serif, bold venue lettering, program hierarchy.
recipes.layout: stacked acts, framed still, showtime block, ticket-like modules.
recipes.imagery: projector, piano, crowd, outlaw scene, curtain, street facade.
recipes.motion: flicker, title cards, reel change, iris transition.
recipes.risk: using 1920s Hollywood glamour before it exists.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: small-theater scale and short-to-long program transition.

promptSeeds:
Design this through a 1906 lens: Gaudi has begun Casa Mila in Barcelona while
Vienna Secession and Wiener Werkstätte designers are tightening ornament into
rectangles, furniture, and coordinated rooms. Make the result Belle Epoque but
structural, not merely floral.

Give me three 1906 directions:
1. Casa Mila organism
2. Secession room discipline
3. Poster attention machine
For each, explain type, color, material, composition, and what would be
anachronistic.

Critique this interface as if it were an exhibition poster or program from 1906.
Does it read at street speed, belong to a total interior, or rely on later
modernist minimalism that has not arrived yet?

objects: Wiener Werkstätte furniture, metalwork, textiles, and tableware
Thonet bentwood chairs in cafes and public interiors
Early safety razors, gramophones, cameras, and typewriters
Fashion plates and hats of the Belle Epoque silhouette
Handcrafted Arts and Crafts domestic objects
printAndGraphics: Leonetto Cappiello advertising posters
Vienna Secession and Wiener Werkstätte printed matter
Die Brücke early woodcuts and announcements
Art Nouveau and Jugendstil magazines and exhibition posters
Programs and posters for early cinema venues
spaces: Casa Mila, Barcelona, begun 1906 by Antoni Gaudi
Sanatorium Purkersdorf near Vienna by Josef Hoffmann
Palais Stoclet in Brussels, under construction
Paris and Vienna poster-lined boulevards and shop windows
Nickelodeons and small film theaters

antiCliches: A generic Art Nouveau screensaver of endless vines
Pure Bauhaus minimalism before the Bauhaus exists
Jazz-age Deco with chrome, zigzags, and skyscraper glamour
Gaudi as fantasy illustration without construction, city, or stone
Vienna as only Klimt gold, ignoring Hoffmann's black-and-white discipline
Early cinema as a grand palace instead of modest commercial venues
Arts and Crafts nostalgia with no branded products or urban advertising

sectionMap.label: Graphic design

sectionMap.label: Product design

sectionMap.label: Architecture
sectionMap.description: Casa Mila is the central architectural signal of 1906.

sectionMap.label: Fashion
sectionMap.description: The fashionable body in 1906 is still Belle Epoque: corseted waist, dramatic hat, long skir...

sectionMap.label: Music
sectionMap.description: 1906 music sits between impressionist atmosphere and theatrical public life.

sectionMap.label: Film
sectionMap.description: 1906 is important because cinema begins to prove it can sustain longer narrative attention.

sectionMap.label: Surface
sectionMap.description: 1906 surfaces are tactile and divided.

sectionMap.label: Anti-cliches
sectionMap.description: Do not make 1906 look like:

examples.title: 1907: industry gets a conscience
examples.subtitle: The Deutscher Werkbund forms, Peter Behrens joins AEG, and Picasso's studio rupture suggests that modern form can be corporate, geometric, and violently new at once.
examples.feeling: the hand, the factory, and the fractured figure entering the same argument
primaryLens: the deutscher werkbund links artists, architects, craft, and industry
peter behrens begins turning aeg into an early corporate design system
picasso's les demoiselles d'avignon breaks figure and space into modern fracture
art nouveau gives ground to organization, identity, and industrial purpose
the poster and exhibition remain laboratories for public modernity

recipes.name: Early corporate electricity
recipes.useFor: technology brands, infrastructure, utilities, product systems, institutional campaigns.
recipes.palette: electrical black, porcelain white, brass, industrial green, warm paper.
recipes.type: sturdy capitals, rational serif, early logo discipline.
recipes.layout: centered mark, ordered catalogue grid, product series, specification blocks.
recipes.imagery: lamps, turbines, wires, switches, factory silhouettes, glow halos.
recipes.motion: current pulse, switch-on reveal, diagram lines connecting products.
recipes.risk: making it look like 1950s corporate identity instead of 1907 industrial reform.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: Behrens and AEG references from the appointment year.

recipes.name: Werkbund quality argument
recipes.useFor: manufacturing, tools, public policy, educational design, exhibitions.
recipes.palette: cream, charcoal, muted red, steel grey, dark green.
recipes.type: clear serif or early sans-like display, minimal ornament.
recipes.layout: comparison tables, catalog panels, product families, disciplined captions.
recipes.imagery: workshop, factory, material samples, model objects, exhibition cases.
recipes.motion: assembly of parts into a coherent series, measured not frantic.
recipes.risk: confusing Werkbund reform with later Bauhaus reduction.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: craft-and-industry tension, not pure machine worship.

recipes.name: Cubist rupture seed
recipes.useFor: art direction, editorial, cultural critique, experimental identity.
recipes.palette: ochre, rose, charcoal, bone, deep blue.
recipes.type: restrained captions so the fractured image carries the shock.
recipes.layout: compressed space, faceted figure, shallow field, mask-like frontal force.
recipes.imagery: bodies, masks, studio planes, angular still-life fragments.
recipes.motion: planes sliding, viewpoints colliding, abrupt faceted cuts.
recipes.risk: using fully developed Synthetic Cubism before 1912.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: Les Demoiselles d'Avignon as a raw 1907 rupture, not polished Cubist branding.

recipes.name: Secession under industry
recipes.useFor: luxury technology, museums, stationery, interiors, cultural institutions.
recipes.palette: black, ivory, muted gold, violet brown, grey green.
recipes.type: spaced capitals, refined serif, decorative but controlled initials.
recipes.layout: gridded panels, frame within frame, motif repetition, calm margins.
recipes.imagery: squares, abstract florals, metal fittings, tiled walls, furniture silhouettes.
recipes.motion: slow panel sequencing, precise alignment, crafted transitions.
recipes.risk: erasing the industrial pressure that makes 1907 distinct.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: a visible negotiation between handcraft and repeatability.

promptSeeds:
Design this through a 1907 lens: the Deutscher Werkbund has just formed, Peter
Behrens has become artistic adviser to AEG, and Picasso has painted Les
Demoiselles d'Avignon. Build a direction where craft reform, corporate identity,
and fractured modern form are distinct but related.

Give me three 1907-informed routes:
1. Early corporate electricity
2. Werkbund quality argument
3. Cubist rupture seed
For each, explain historical lineage, type, material, color, and risks.

Critique this product system as if it were presented in 1907. Does it show
Werkbund-quality thinking, AEG-style identity, or merely decorative Art Nouveau
surface?

objects: AEG electrical products associated with Peter Behrens's design program
Wiener Werkstätte furniture, metalwork, and textiles
Thonet bentwood chairs and industrially repeated cafe furniture
Early domestic electrical appliances, lamps, switches, and fans
Arts and Crafts and Werkbund exhibition-quality manufactured goods
printAndGraphics: Deutscher Werkbund founding documents and early publications
AEG marks, catalogues, advertisements, and product graphics by Peter Behrens
Picasso's Les Demoiselles d'Avignon as a formal rupture
Cappiello and other simplified Paris advertising posters
Die Brücke woodcuts, posters, and announcements
spaces: Munich context of the Deutscher Werkbund founding
AEG industrial and administrative environments in Berlin
Palais Stoclet under construction in Brussels
Casa Mila under construction in Barcelona
Dresden studios associated with Die Brücke

antiCliches: Finished Bauhaus minimalism fifteen years too early
Generic Art Nouveau vines with no Werkbund or AEG industrial pressure
1920s corporate identity with Helvetica-like neutrality
Fully developed Cubism with pasted newspaper and synthetic collage
Steampunk fantasy instead of electrical industry and catalog order
Vienna reduced to gold decoration without production discipline
A poster-only year, ignoring products, factories, and institutions

examples.title: 1908: ornament on trial
examples.subtitle: Loos attacks ornament, Braque's L'Estaque paintings push form toward cubes, and the Ford Model T makes industrial modernity ordinary, affordable, and terrifyingly repeatable.
examples.feeling: the decorative century being cross-examined by cubes, cars, and plain walls
primaryLens: ornament and crime turns decoration into a moral argument
cubism begins to name a new geometry of perception
the ford model t makes mass industrial design a public fact
aeg and the werkbund connect identity, factories, and standardization
fauvist color and arts and crafts material honesty remain active counterforces

recipes.name: Ornament on trial
recipes.useFor: architecture critique, editorial, cultural institutions, reform brands.
recipes.palette: marble white, walnut, black, oxblood, muted brass.
recipes.type: sober serif, widely spaced capitals, minimal flourish.
recipes.layout: severe margins, material swatches, before-and-after contrasts, plain frames.
recipes.imagery: smooth walls, marble veining, mirror, chair, doorway, rejected ornament.
recipes.motion: stripping away layers, reveal of material, slow proportional cuts.
recipes.risk: mistaking Loos for cold minimalism with no luxury or sensual material.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: Vienna, 1908 lecture context, and real material richness.

recipes.name: Model T pragmatism
recipes.useFor: mobility, tools, repair, public services, manufacturing, logistics.
recipes.palette: black, road dust, steel grey, cream paper, oil brown.
recipes.type: practical serif, manual typography, strong product name.
recipes.layout: parts diagram, price and utility hierarchy, dealer notice, catalogue page.
recipes.imagery: chassis, wheel, road, wrench, farm, town street, driver.
recipes.motion: crank, wheel rotation, assembly rhythm, road dust wipe.
recipes.risk: using later assembly-line imagery as if it already defines 1908.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: introduction-year affordability and repair logic, not 1913 line mythology.

recipes.name: Early Cubist construction
recipes.useFor: art direction, editorial covers, cultural analysis, experimental campaigns.
recipes.palette: ochre, grey green, umber, black, pale sky blue.
recipes.type: restrained serif or hand label kept secondary to image structure.
recipes.layout: shallow space, faceted planes, compressed houses, multiple angles.
recipes.imagery: L'Estaque houses, trees, table objects, masks, angular bodies.
recipes.motion: planes tilting, space folding, viewpoint shifts without collage.
recipes.risk: using later newspaper collage, bright Synthetic Cubism, or 1920s geometry.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: Braque's 1908 L'Estaque landscapes and Vauxcelles' "cubes" context.

recipes.name: AEG industrial trust
recipes.useFor: energy, engineering, appliances, infrastructure, enterprise tools.
recipes.palette: black, porcelain, electric green, brass, graphite.
recipes.type: rational capitals, early corporate mark, catalogue hierarchy.
recipes.layout: product family grid, technical captions, consistent logo placement.
recipes.imagery: lamps, fans, turbines, switches, factory frame, electrical glow.
recipes.motion: switch-on pulse, rotating turbine, line diagram animation.
recipes.risk: making the identity too clean and late-modern.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: Behrens's early AEG work and Werkbund reform context.

promptSeeds:
Design this through a 1908 lens: Loos has delivered Ornament and Crime, Braque's
L'Estaque paintings are pushing toward Cubism, and the Ford Model T has appeared.
Make the work feel like ornament under trial by material, mass production, and
geometric perception.

Give me four 1908 directions:
1. Ornament on trial
2. Model T pragmatism
3. Early Cubist construction
4. AEG industrial trust
For each, define type, color, layout, material, motion, and anachronism risks.

Critique this product page as if it were made in 1908. Does it rely on Art
Nouveau decoration, Werkbund industrial clarity, Loos material restraint, or a
Model T-style promise of practical mass use?

Loos interiors using marble, wood, mirror, and proportion

printAndGraphics: Adolf Loos's "Ornament and Crime" lecture and later essay tradition

AEG catalogues, marks, and advertisements by Peter Behrens

spaces: Loos's Kärntner Bar, later called the American Bar, Vienna

Fully developed Cubist collage from 1912 or later

examples.title: 1909: speed enters the room
examples.subtitle: Marinetti publishes the Futurist Manifesto, the Ballets Russes detonates color in Paris, and industrial architecture learns to look like power itself.
examples.feeling: speed and color claiming the future before anyone has agreed what the future is
primaryLens: futurism makes speed, machines, violence, and youth into a design myth
the ballets russes brings russian color, costume, and stage design to paris
aeg and behrens give industrial architecture monumental corporate form
frank lloyd wright's prairie house stretches domestic modernity horizontally
poiret pushes fashion toward uncorseted theatrical self-design

recipes.name: Futurist manifesto spark
recipes.useFor: campaigns, launches, music, editorial shocks, motion identities.
recipes.palette: newspaper cream, black ink, danger red, steel grey, headlamp yellow.
recipes.type: oversized serif and display fragments, shouting hierarchy, diagonal emphasis.
recipes.layout: manifesto block, abrupt breaks, speed vectors, compressed headline force.
recipes.imagery: automobile, streetlamp, crowd, smoke, wheels, newspaper front page.
recipes.motion: acceleration, impact cuts, repeated words, vibrating lines.
recipes.risk: sanitizing Futurism's violent politics into harmless racing graphics.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: Marinetti's 20 February 1909 Le Figaro publication context.

recipes.name: Ballets Russes color stage
recipes.useFor: fashion, performance, beauty, hospitality, music, exhibition design.
recipes.palette: turquoise, saffron, vermilion, purple, black, gold.
recipes.type: theatrical serif, elegant program hierarchy, hand-drawn accent lettering.
recipes.layout: central dancer, curtain frame, costume silhouette, layered textiles.
recipes.imagery: Bakst costume, drapery, jewels, stage flats, dancers, orientalized fantasy.
recipes.motion: curtain rise, spinning fabric, footlight glow, choreographic sweeps.
recipes.risk: using vague exoticism without acknowledging Ballets Russes specificity and its orientalist framing.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: Diaghilev's 1909 Paris debut and Bakst's early designs.

recipes.name: AEG turbine monument
recipes.useFor: infrastructure, energy, engineering, civic technology, industrial brands.
recipes.palette: steel grey, glass green, brick, black enamel, warm electric light.
recipes.type: disciplined capitals, early corporate mark, technical catalogue text.
recipes.layout: monumental gable, repeated bays, product-system grid, symmetric power.
recipes.imagery: turbine hall, steel frame, crane, lamp, cable, factory window.
recipes.motion: turbine rotation, electrical pulse, doors opening like a civic facade.
recipes.risk: making it look like later brutalism or mid-century corporate modernism.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: Peter Behrens's AEG program and 1909 turbine factory completion.

recipes.name: Prairie horizontal home

recipes.name: Futurist manifesto
recipes.useFor: launches, campaigns, music identities, editorial attacks, activist design.

recipes.layout: central figure, ornamental border, costume silhouette, program-like hierarchy.
recipes.imagery: dancers, veils, feathers, jewels, painted flats, stage curtains.
recipes.motion: curtain reveal, fabric sweep, fan-like color transitions.
recipes.risk: vague "Orientalist" fantasy without naming the stage-design source.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: Bakst-like costume logic and clear performance framing.

recipes.type: restrained serif or early sans-serif, quiet hierarchy.
recipes.layout: flat elevation, severe margins, few decorative moves, proportion as ornament.
recipes.imagery: smooth walls, windows, thresholds, stairs, domestic objects.
recipes.motion: slow reveal, shadow crossing plain surface, measured cuts.
recipes.risk: making 1910 look like late modern minimalism.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: Viennese material weight and pre-Bauhaus restraint.

recipes.name: Werkbund standard
recipes.useFor: design systems, manufacturing, civic products, architecture practices, standards documentation.
recipes.palette: warm paper, black, factory grey, glass green, brick red.
recipes.type: condensed poster titles with sober serif explanations.
recipes.layout: catalogue grid, product typologies, elevation plus specification.
recipes.imagery: model factory, exhibition halls, product displays, diagrams, glass walls.
recipes.motion: modular assembly, parts aligning, catalogue-to-building reveal.
recipes.risk: making 1914 look like finished Bauhaus.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: Muthesius versus van de Velde and Cologne exhibition context.

recipes.name: Kitchener command poster
recipes.useFor: campaigns, public service messages, political history, museum interpretation.
recipes.palette: cream paper, black, khaki, flag red, navy blue.
recipes.type: heavy condensed display, direct imperative, minimal supporting text.
recipes.layout: frontal figure, pointing gesture, large command, strong border.
recipes.imagery: uniform, finger point, national symbols, enlistment office, newspaper cover.
recipes.motion: direct gaze, snap zoom, command card reveal.
recipes.risk: treating propaganda as neutral vintage decoration.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: 1914 recruitment context and the ethics of persuasion.

recipes.name: Vorticist blast
recipes.useFor: experimental publishing, music posters, art collectives, critical interfaces.
recipes.palette: black, white, hot pink, dirty cream, iron grey.
recipes.type: huge slab display, abrupt spacing, manifesto lists, violent emphasis.
recipes.layout: angular blocks, blast/bless lists, asymmetry, compressed margins.
recipes.imagery: vortex forms, machines, cities, fragments, aggressive word fields.
recipes.motion: hard cuts, page slams, angular wipes, typographic impact.
recipes.risk: confusing Vorticism with later punk zines.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: BLAST issue one and Wyndham Lewis's typographic rhetoric.

recipes.name: Glass pavilion utopia
recipes.useFor: architecture, light installations, wellness spaces, cultural exhibitions.
recipes.palette: pale glass green, cobalt, amber, white, black shadow.
recipes.type: refined architectural captions, restrained exhibition labels.
recipes.layout: crystalline symmetry, circular plan cues, stair and dome geometry.
recipes.imagery: colored glass, facets, light wells, prismatic reflections, pavilion thresholds.
recipes.motion: light refraction, slow rotation, color passing through glass.
recipes.risk: making it look like contemporary parametric architecture.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: Bruno Taut, Cologne 1914, and prewar utopian glass culture.

recipes.name: Readymade construction
recipes.useFor: product critique, conceptual brands, galleries, object studies, industrial culture.
recipes.palette: metal grey, wood brown, black, cream, dull green.
recipes.type: object-label serif, inventory numbers, spare captions.
recipes.layout: isolated object, wall relief, shelf, catalogue note, negative space.
recipes.imagery: bottle rack, relief materials, wood, metal, wire, studio wall.
recipes.motion: object rotation, selection gesture, shadow shift, assembly reveal.
recipes.risk: jumping ahead to fully developed conceptual art.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: Duchamp's 1914 Bottle Rack and Tatlin's early reliefs.

promptSeeds:
Design this through a 1914 lens: the Cologne Werkbund exhibition has made
standardization a public design argument, World War I has begun turning posters
into mobilization tools, and BLAST has made typography feel violent. Keep these
forces historically distinct.

Give me four 1914-informed directions:
1. Werkbund standard
2. Kitchener command poster
3. Vorticist blast
4. Glass pavilion utopia
For each, explain source, typography, color, material, motion, and ethical risk.

Critique this interface as if it belonged to 1914. Is it standardization,
wartime persuasion, Vorticist typography, glass utopia, or readymade object
logic? What details prove the year?

objects: Duchamp's Bottle Rack
Tatlin's early reliefs and counter-relief experiments
Werkbund product displays and typified industrial goods
Military uniforms, recruitment materials, maps, and insignia
Chaplin's Tramp costume elements: bowler, cane, moustache, shoes
printAndGraphics: Cologne Werkbund exhibition catalogues and architectural publications
Alfred Leete's Lord Kitchener image in London Opinion and recruitment-poster adaptations
Wyndham Lewis's BLAST No. 1
Futurist and Vorticist manifestos and reviews
War maps, newspaper front pages, enlistment notices, and early propaganda posters
spaces: Deutscher Werkbund exhibition grounds in Cologne
Bruno Taut's Glass Pavilion
Gropius and Meyer's model factory at Cologne
Van de Velde's Werkbund theater
Recruitment offices, railway stations, and public streets after mobilization

antiCliches: Fully formed Bauhaus modernism
Red-black Soviet constructivism from the 1920s
Generic World War I sepia trenches only
Art Deco geometry
Futurism as clean science-fiction speed
Vorticism as punk collage without Edwardian print context
War posters treated as harmless vintage decor
A seamless modernist timeline with no rupture
Chaplin nostalgia detached from 1914 screen-industry context

examples.title: 1915: zero and thunder
examples.subtitle: Black Square enters the icon corner, Futurism turns war and speed into a visual grammar, and public design learns that modern life needs stronger signs, posters, and systems.
examples.feeling: the page as battlefield and icon wall
primaryLens: suprematism makes non-objective painting feel like a new visual law
futurism treats speed, noise, machines, and war as compositional forces
wartime posters turn illustration and direct address into mass persuasion
the London Underground begins moving toward a unified typographic identity
exposition architecture still offers Beaux-Arts grandeur as modern spectacle

recipes.name: Suprematist zero
recipes.useFor: galleries, launches, manifestos, abstract brands, cultural institutions.
recipes.palette: black, warm white, deep red, muted ochre, smoky grey.
recipes.type: sparse serif or severe sans, small captions, large geometric silence.
recipes.layout: floating square, corner tension, asymmetry, empty field as active space.
recipes.imagery: black square, circle, cross, angled plane, icon-wall placement.
recipes.motion: slow placement, sudden cut to black, geometric drift.
recipes.risk: generic minimalism with no spiritual or historical charge.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: icon-corner logic and handmade edge, not perfect vector sterility.

recipes.name: Futurist noise page
recipes.useFor: performance, music, campaigns, editorial openers, kinetic identities.
recipes.palette: black, red, cream, steel blue, dirty yellow.
recipes.type: mixed scale, diagonals, bold caps, words as sound effects.
recipes.layout: collision, radial bursts, off-axis fragments, compressed margins.
recipes.imagery: speed lines, machines, crowds, artillery-like rhythm, urban movement.
recipes.motion: staccato cuts, vibrating type, accelerating diagonals.
recipes.risk: comic-book chaos without Futurist structure.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: words-in-freedom and real typographic hierarchy underneath the noise.

recipes.name: Wartime command poster
recipes.useFor: civic campaigns, public-service messages, recruitment metaphors, urgent launches.
recipes.palette: flag red, navy, khaki, black, aged paper.
recipes.type: large imperative capitals, compact slogans, hand-lettered authority.
recipes.layout: single figure, direct gaze, headline first, emblem or seal below.
recipes.imagery: pointing hands, uniforms, factories, flags, ships, bonds.
recipes.motion: hard zoom, poster slap, command reveal.
recipes.risk: glorifying war or flattening propaganda into costume.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: sober acknowledgement that persuasion can be coercive and harmful.

recipes.name: Underground civic clarity
recipes.useFor: transit, wayfinding, public tools, maps, infrastructure brands.
recipes.palette: cream, black, signal red, deep blue, tile white.
recipes.type: humanist sans, clear spacing, consistent station-name logic.
recipes.layout: repeated signs, roundels, modular panels, poster frames.
recipes.imagery: platforms, tiled walls, routes, arrows, city movement.
recipes.motion: train arrival, sign alignment, map-line tracing.
recipes.risk: importing later Transport for London polish too early.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: 1915 as commission and beginning, not the fully mature system.

promptSeeds:
Design this through a 1915 lens: Malevich has just shown Black Square at 0.10,
Futurist typography is making the page sound like machinery, and war posters are
turning public walls into command surfaces. Keep abstraction, noise, and propaganda
morally distinct.

Give me three 1915-informed directions:
1. Suprematist zero
2. Futurist noise page
3. Underground civic clarity
For each, explain the historical lineage, typography, palette, material surface,
and what to avoid.

Critique this poster as if it appeared in 1915. Is it an avant-garde abstraction,
a wartime command image, an exposition spectacle, or a public-transport system?
What evidence supports that reading?

Film palaces and nickelodeons showing feature-length spectacle

antiCliches: Fully polished Bauhaus minimalism
1920s Art Deco luxury
Generic sepia war nostalgia
Futurism without its disturbing militarist charge
Suprematism as blank corporate minimalism
Transport design with late-20th-century signage perfection
Silent film reduced to cute title-card pastiche
Exposition architecture without scale, plaster, light, and civic theater

examples.title: 1916: cabaret and clarity
examples.subtitle: Dada begins in neutral Zurich as nonsense against war, while Edward Johnston gives the Underground a disciplined alphabet for modern public life.
examples.feeling: nonsense against war, clarity against chaos
primaryLens: dada is born at Cabaret Voltaire as anti-art, performance, and protest
public lettering becomes modern, legible, and humane through Johnston Sans
wartime graphic systems intensify through recruitment, bonds, and morale
city planning and public agencies begin treating design as governance
abstraction becomes both spiritual refuge and political refusal

recipes.name: Cabaret rupture
recipes.useFor: experimental arts, music venues, radical editorial, performance identities.
recipes.palette: black, cream, blood red, cardboard brown, smoky grey.
recipes.type: mismatched display, abrupt caps, handbills, typewriter fragments.
recipes.layout: collage, interruptions, odd spacing, stage-program logic.
recipes.imagery: masks, cardboard costumes, cut paper, small stage, multilingual fragments.
recipes.motion: sudden entrances, jump cuts, syllable bursts, applause turning into noise.
recipes.risk: cute nonsense with no anti-war or anti-bourgeois edge.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: performance residue - make it feel printed after a night in a small room.

recipes.name: Johnston civic alphabet
recipes.useFor: transit systems, public tools, city services, wayfinding, institutional identity.
recipes.palette: tile white, deep blue, signal red, black, enamel cream.
recipes.type: humanist sans, generous spacing, round forms, clear station naming.
recipes.layout: repeated panels, route hierarchy, map-adjacent order, poster frames.
recipes.imagery: platforms, roundels, tiled corridors, tickets, signs.
recipes.motion: line tracing, sign alignment, station-to-station rhythm.
recipes.risk: making it look like contemporary transport branding.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: Johnston's calligraphic warmth and early-20th-century enamel material.

recipes.name: Zoning diagram city
recipes.useFor: urban planning, civic tech, policy explainers, architecture studios.
recipes.palette: blueprint blue, paper cream, black rule, muted red, graphite grey.
recipes.type: condensed labels, annotation, official captions, table headings.
recipes.layout: setbacks, sections, height envelopes, grid streets, numbered zones.
recipes.imagery: tower silhouettes, street canyons, planning maps, civic seals.
recipes.motion: extruded massing, stepped setbacks, overlay comparisons.
recipes.risk: using later skyscraper Deco instead of regulatory logic.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: diagrams that explain rules rather than decorate the skyline.

recipes.name: War-office poster
recipes.useFor: urgent civic campaigns, crisis communication, public health, institutional appeals.
recipes.palette: khaki, black, red, navy, worn paper.
recipes.type: imperative headlines, official subheads, seal or unit mark.
recipes.layout: figure plus command, large negative shape, small official information.
recipes.imagery: workers, soldiers, nurses, ships, fields, factories.
recipes.motion: poster pasted to wall, headline appearing first, emblem stamp.
recipes.risk: romanticizing war mobilization.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: restrained urgency and visible print grain.

promptSeeds:
Design this through a 1916 lens: Cabaret Voltaire has just made nonsense into an

What evidence supports the answer?

examples.title: 1917: revolution gets a grid
examples.subtitle: De Stijl begins in the Netherlands, Duchamp turns a urinal into a question, Russia enters revolution, and the Uncle Sam poster proves direct address can become national iconography.
examples.feeling: the future trying to draw itself as a hard line

jazz enters commercial recording and changes the tempo of modern culture

recipes.name: De Stijl beginning
recipes.useFor: systems, cultural brands, editorial design, architecture concepts, educational tools.
recipes.palette: white, black, red, yellow, blue, warm grey.
recipes.type: simple sans or restrained serif, structured captions, clear modular scale.
recipes.layout: verticals, horizontals, rectangular fields, asymmetrical balance.
recipes.imagery: planes, grids, chair joints, magazine pages, abstract compositions.
recipes.motion: sliding planes, snap alignment, color block sequencing.
recipes.risk: using later generic Mondrian decor without 1917's theoretical seriousness.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: magazine logic and early movement formation, not mature branding shorthand.

recipes.name: Revolutionary broadside
recipes.useFor: activism, public statements, urgent editorial, social campaigns.
recipes.palette: red, black, off-white, dirty grey, dark brown.
recipes.type: heavy headline, newspaper texture, condensed emphasis, rough alignment.
recipes.layout: stacked notices, diagonal pressure, crowd-readable hierarchy.
recipes.imagery: workers, crowds, flags, presses, factories, street meetings.
recipes.motion: paper flood, headline slam, marching rhythm.
recipes.risk: empty revolutionary cosplay.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: political purpose and print scarcity, not decorative red stars from later decades.

recipes.name: Direct-address recruitment
recipes.useFor: public campaigns, accountability tools, civic prompts, theatrical posters.
recipes.palette: navy, red, cream, black, uniform khaki.
recipes.type: short imperative caps, wide spacing, official subtext.
recipes.layout: figure centered, hand or gaze breaking the viewer's distance, slogan below.
recipes.imagery: pointing figure, uniform, flag, seal, enlistment office.
recipes.motion: gaze lock, pointing gesture, poster close-up.
recipes.risk: adopting propaganda force without ethical framing.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: Flagg-like directness and acknowledgement of coercive design.

recipes.name: Ready-made question
recipes.useFor: conceptual art, product critique, museums, design ethics, object-led campaigns.
recipes.palette: porcelain white, black, label cream, gallery grey, ink blue.
recipes.type: title card, signature, catalogue entry, institutional label.
recipes.layout: object plus context, empty display field, caption as detonator.
recipes.imagery: ordinary manufactured object, signature, plinth, exhibition refusal.
recipes.motion: rotate object, reveal title, cut to label.
recipes.risk: treating Dada as a prank without institutional critique.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: selection, naming, and rejection as the design mechanism.

Design this through a 1917 lens: De Stijl has just begun, Duchamp's Fountain has

Critique this campaign as if it appeared in 1917. Is it Flagg-like direct address,

examples.title: 1918: white square, armistice light
examples.subtitle: The war ends, De Stijl publishes its manifesto, Malevich paints white on white, and the modern surface becomes austere, exhausted, and newly absolute.
examples.feeling: a world ending in noise while abstraction lowers its voice to white
primaryLens: the Armistice turns propaganda from mobilization toward victory, mourning, and reconstruction
De Stijl states its program as a manifesto of new visual order
Malevich's White on White pushes abstraction toward near-disappearance
wartime austerity makes material economy and graphic force unavoidable
influenza, ruin, and demobilization darken every promise of modernity

recipes.name: White-on-white abstraction
recipes.useFor: galleries, quiet interfaces, memorials, luxury restraint, conceptual identities.
recipes.palette: warm white, cold white, pale grey, black pinline, faint ochre.
recipes.type: minimal captions, small serif or sans, lots of deliberate silence.
recipes.layout: off-axis square, barely visible contrast, surface as event.
recipes.imagery: tilted white plane, paint texture, shadow edge, empty field.
recipes.motion: slow tonal shift, square emerging from ground, quiet rotation.
recipes.risk: sterile minimalism with no Suprematist intensity.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: painted irregularity and the sense that whiteness is an argument.

recipes.name: Armistice wall
recipes.useFor: memorials, civic campaigns, historical exhibitions, public-service design.
recipes.palette: flag red, navy, cream, black, khaki, faded gold.
recipes.type: official capitals, serif announcements, poster slogans, dated notices.
recipes.layout: pasted layers, bond appeals, relief notices, flags, casualty lists.
recipes.imagery: crowds, soldiers returning, poppies, ships, bells, documents.
recipes.motion: paper layers peeling, headline changes from war to peace, bell rhythm.
recipes.risk: sentimental victory imagery without grief or exhaustion.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: relief, mourning, and reconstruction mixed with celebration.

recipes.name: De Stijl manifesto page

recipes.layout: vertical-horizontal balance, generous margins, diagrammatic sequence.

recipes.motion: stamps, sorting, map routing, form completion.
recipes.risk: making bureaucracy look clean and cheerful.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: material scarcity and human aftermath.

examples.title: 1924: manifestos before the fair
examples.subtitle: Surrealism names the unconscious, Rietveld builds De Stijl into a house, and modernism prepares the arguments that Paris will stage one year later.
examples.feeling: the dream and the grid waiting for Paris
primaryLens: Surrealism publishes its first manifesto and gives design a dream logic to resist pure rationalism
Rietveld's Schroder House turns De Stijl planes into built domestic space
constructivism, Bauhaus practice, and machine rhetoric keep hardening modern graphic language
jazz-age fashion, radio, cinema, and magazines make style faster, younger, and more public
decorative modernism gathers the motifs that will become spectacular in 1925

recipes.name: Schroder planes
recipes.useFor: architecture, modular systems, furniture, spatial interfaces, design education.
recipes.palette: white, black, grey, red, yellow, blue.
recipes.type: spare sans or block lettering subordinate to plane and line.
recipes.layout: asymmetrical planes, open corners, sliding panels, color accents.
recipes.imagery: rails, windows, chairs, partitions, exterior planes, axonometric views.
recipes.motion: sliding wall, rotating plane, color block shifting in space.
recipes.risk: flat Mondrian parody with no domestic or spatial logic.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: flexible living space and De Stijl relation.

recipes.name: Surrealist manifesto
recipes.useFor: arts organizations, dream journals, experimental products, film titles.
recipes.palette: cream paper, black ink, blood red, cloudy grey, midnight blue.
recipes.type: literary serif disrupted by abrupt sans or typewriter insertions.
recipes.layout: manifesto page, unexpected image-text collision, generous uneasy margins.
recipes.imagery: dream fragments, masks, eyes, rooms, hands, automatic marks.
recipes.motion: association jump, dissolve, uncanny cut, phrase turning into object.
recipes.risk: later Dali melting-clock cliche.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: Breton's 1924 manifesto context and automatic writing.

recipes.name: Pre-Exposition Deco
recipes.useFor: hospitality, fashion, beauty, travel, theater, packaging.
recipes.palette: black, gold, cream, jade, coral, lapis.
recipes.type: bold display capitals, fat faces, geometric ornaments.
recipes.layout: symmetrical poster, stepped frame, sunburst hints, central figure.
recipes.imagery: stylized flowers, fans, fountains, dancers, cosmetics, city lights.
recipes.motion: curtain reveal, fan opening, stepped ascent, spotlight.
recipes.risk: using fully codified 1925 exposition glamour too early.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: transitional ornament and exhibition anticipation.

recipes.name: Rhapsody city
recipes.useFor: music brands, city campaigns, event identities, motion graphics.
recipes.palette: blue, black, cream, brass, smoky grey, taxi yellow.
recipes.type: jazz-age display with editorial serif support.
recipes.layout: skyline rhythm, music-staff lines, traffic-like syncopation.
recipes.imagery: clarinet glissando, train, skyline, theater lights, sheet music.
recipes.motion: rising glissando, syncopated cuts, city-pan reveal.
recipes.risk: generic jazz wallpaper.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: Gershwin's 1924 concert-jazz urbanity.

promptSeeds:
Design this through a 1924 lens: Breton has just published the Surrealist
Manifesto, Rietveld and Truus Schroder-Schrader have built the Schroder House,
and decorative modernism is gathering force before the 1925 Paris Exposition.

Give me three 1924-informed directions:
1. Schroder planes
2. Surrealist manifesto
3. Pre-Exposition Deco
For each, explain source, typography, palette, spatial logic, motion, and what
to avoid.

Critique this layout as if it appeared in 1924. Is it De Stijl architecture,
Surrealist manifesto culture, constructivist graphics, or pre-Exposition Deco?
What evidence supports that lineage?

objects: Gerrit Rietveld furniture and built-in elements associated with the Schroder House
Bauhaus workshop objects from the Weimar period
Radios, records, cosmetics, and cigarette cases for jazz-age domestic life
Sport medals, programs, and objects associated with the 1924 Winter Olympics
Sheet music and recordings connected to Gershwin's Rhapsody in Blue
printAndGraphics: Andre Breton's first Manifesto of Surrealism
De Stijl publications and Rietveld/Schroder House drawings

spaces: The Rietveld Schroder House in Utrecht
Bauhaus classrooms and workshops in Weimar
Wembley exhibition grounds and pavilions
Radio listening rooms, cinemas, and jazz clubs
Concert halls associated with the premiere of Rhapsody in Blue

antiCliches: A 1925 Paris Exposition recap
Later Surrealism reduced to melting clocks
Generic Deco skyscraper style from the late 1920s or 1930s
Bauhaus Dessau glass curtain walls before the move
De Stijl as flat poster blocks with no spatial or domestic relation
Jazz-age costume without radio, records, sheet music, or city rhythm
Clean functionalism that ignores dream, ornament, and spectacle
Egyptian motifs with no link to the post-1922 craze

examples.title: 1925: the year design gets a name
examples.subtitle: Paris stages the exposition that will later be called Art Deco, the Bauhaus reopens in Dessau, and luxury ornament and machine geometry argue in public for the soul of the modern object.
examples.feeling: luxury and the machine arguing over the same future
primaryLens: the Paris Exposition turns decorative modernity into an international style
the Bauhaus moves to Dessau and rebuilds modernism as a working method
constructivism and De Stijl push geometry, machine logic, and red-black abstraction
jazz, cinema, and the flapper rewrite the body, the night, and the moving image
the machine becomes both a luxury motif and a moral argument about how to live

recipes.name: Deco luxury
recipes.useFor: premium brands, hospitality, beauty, jewelry, theaters, title sequences.
recipes.palette: black, gold, jade, cream, deep lacquer red.
recipes.type: tall condensed capitals, stylized serifs, symmetrical lockups.
recipes.layout: vertical symmetry, stepped frames, central emblems, radiating lines.
recipes.imagery: sunbursts, fountains, gazelles, stylized flora, geometric inlay.
recipes.motion: slow reveal, mirrored symmetry, gilded shimmer.
recipes.risk: looking like a Gatsby party-supply kit.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: real material logic - lacquer, veneer, metal inlay - not just gold gradients.

recipes.name: Bauhaus method
recipes.useFor: tools, education, systems, manufacturing, honest product brands.
recipes.palette: red, blue, yellow, black, and white.
recipes.type: geometric sans, lowercase, asymmetric, grid-aligned.
recipes.layout: modular grid, generous space, structure made visible.
recipes.imagery: circle, square, triangle, photograph, diagram.
recipes.motion: snap to grid, primary-color transitions, mechanical rhythm.
recipes.risk: generic "minimalist" wallpaper with no system underneath.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: a real grid and a real reason for every element.

recipes.name: Constructivist signal
recipes.useFor: campaigns, manifestos, music, activism, bold editorial.
recipes.palette: red, black, off-white.
recipes.type: heavy condensed sans, diagonal, oversized.
recipes.layout: diagonal axes, photomontage, thick bars and rules.
recipes.imagery: workers, machines, cut photography, arrows, megaphones.
recipes.motion: hard cuts, sliding bars, diagonal entrances.
recipes.risk: cosplaying revolution as decoration.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: montage logic and a message worth shouting.

recipes.name: Jazz-age night
recipes.useFor: nightlife, music, events, film, performance brands.
recipes.palette: black, electric gold, spotlight white, deep red.
recipes.type: theatrical display capitals, hand-drawn flourishes.
recipes.layout: poster-first, figure in motion, big name, big night.
recipes.imagery: dancers, silhouettes, spotlights, instruments, city electricity.
recipes.motion: spotlight sweeps, curtain reveals, syncopated timing.
recipes.risk: flat "speakeasy" cliche with no rhythm.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: real performance energy and poster simplification.

promptSeeds:
Design this through a 1925 lens: the Paris Exposition has just turned decorative
modernity into an international style while the Bauhaus reopens in Dessau as a
machine-age method. Give me directions that keep luxury Deco and machine
modernism distinct instead of blending them into generic vintage.

Give me three 1925-informed directions:
1. Deco luxury
2. Bauhaus method
3. Constructivist signal
For each, explain the historical lineage, typography, color, material, and what
to avoid.

Critique this layout as if it appeared in 1925. Is it Deco ornament, Bauhaus
system, or constructivist montage? What evidence of material, grid, or diagonal
supports that lineage?

spaces: The Paris Exposition pavilions along the Seine

Parisian jazz cabarets and revue theaters

antiCliches: A modern "Gatsby" template with plastic gold gradients

Bauhaus reduced to three primary squares and nothing else
Steampunk gears pretending to be machine-age modernism
A jazz cliche with no understanding of poster or performance design
Smooth digital symmetry with none of the era's handcraft or grain

examples.title: 1926: modernism moves into the building

tubular steel furniture shifts from experiment toward industrial language

recipes.name: Dessau system
recipes.useFor: education platforms, design tools, institutional identities, studios, product systems.
recipes.palette: off-white, black, red, blue, yellow, steel grey.
recipes.type: lowercase geometric sans, asymmetric hierarchy, small labels.
recipes.layout: bridge-like blocks, visible grid, functional zones, generous margins.
recipes.imagery: glass walls, workshop tables, diagrams, stairwells, photographs.
recipes.motion: elements slide into alignment, planes reveal function, hard cuts.
recipes.risk: generic Bauhaus cosplay with no program or structure.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: a real relationship between layout, function, and use.

recipes.name: Cantilever object
recipes.useFor: furniture brands, hardware, mobility tools, engineering-led products.
recipes.palette: chrome, black leather, warm neutral walls, industrial grey.
recipes.type: technical sans, concise labels, catalog clarity.
recipes.layout: one continuous line, side elevation, stress and support made visible.
recipes.imagery: tubular steel, bent pipe, shadows under a floating seat.
recipes.motion: spring, flex, hover, chair-line drawing itself in one stroke.
recipes.risk: using later polished mid-century cues without the 1926 experimental roughness.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: structural logic before comfort styling.

recipes.name: Jazz-age black
recipes.useFor: fashion, nightlife, beauty, editorial, performance identities.

recipes.type: elegant Deco caps with restrained serif body text.
recipes.layout: vertical figure, narrow columns, spotlight oval, strong silhouette.
recipes.imagery: bobbed hair, black dress, stage curtain, cigarette, cabaret poster.
recipes.motion: curtain reveal, flashbulb, syncopated cuts, slow turn.
recipes.risk: flattening the year into costume-party flapper imagery.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: restraint, modern cut, and real performance publicity.

recipes.name: Constructed page
recipes.useFor: manifestos, campaigns, editorial systems, posters, cultural programs.

recipes.layout: photomontage, rules, bars, asymmetric axes, active corners.
recipes.imagery: machines, hands, buildings, faces, cropped photographs.
recipes.motion: diagonal sweep, type blocks locking, photographic cuts.
recipes.risk: decorative revolution with no message.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: hierarchy that behaves like argument.
