examples.title: 1927: the line goes public
examples.subtitle: Futura, Weissenhof, Metropolis, the talkie, and the machine-age poster make the future readable as type, housing, speed, sound, and cinematic city.
examples.feeling: the modern line learns to speak to crowds
primaryLens: Futura turns geometric sans-serif into a commercial modern voice
the Weissenhof Estate makes white-wall modern housing public and controversial
Metropolis gives the machine city its most influential cinematic image
synchronized dialogue changes film, publicity, theater design, and popular attention
posters and transport graphics compress speed into monumental simplified form

recipes.name: Futura public
recipes.useFor: civic brands, transport systems, technology launches, editorial identity, wayfinding.
recipes.palette: cream, black, deep teal, rail blue, signal red.
recipes.type: geometric sans, circular counters, crisp hierarchy, confident spacing.
recipes.layout: centered or asymmetric but rigorously aligned, type as structure.
recipes.imagery: circles, tracks, signs, plans, measuring lines.
recipes.motion: letters assemble from compass arcs and straight rules.
recipes.risk: using later corporate minimalism instead of 1927 geometric optimism.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: print texture and typefoundry discipline.

recipes.name: Weissenhof white
recipes.useFor: housing, architecture studios, interiors, urban planning, social design.
recipes.palette: white, warm grey, black, muted green, steel.
recipes.type: clean sans with plan labels and measured captions.
recipes.layout: terrace, strip window, block, module, elevation and plan together.
recipes.imagery: flat roofs, balconies, tubular chairs, sunlit walls, open rooms.
recipes.motion: walking promenade, sliding room sequence, window-band reveal.
recipes.risk: turning early modern housing into sterile luxury minimalism.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: social housing questions, not just white boxes.

recipes.name: Machine-city cinema
recipes.useFor: films, games, AI systems, infrastructure brands, speculative interfaces.
recipes.palette: black, silver, smoke, electric white, warning red.
recipes.type: monumental capitals, mechanical labels, high contrast.
recipes.layout: vertical towers, repeated workers, machine circles, deep perspective.
recipes.imagery: elevators, gears, city canyons, robot figure, crowd choreography.
recipes.motion: synchronized labor, pulsing machinery, hard light, vertical ascent.
recipes.risk: generic dystopian skyline without 1927 theatrical construction.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: miniature, set, shadow, and crowd logic.

recipes.name: Talkie threshold
recipes.useFor: audio products, podcasts, cinema, performance tools, voice interfaces.
recipes.palette: theater red, black, brass, cream, carbon grey.
recipes.type: poster display with sound promises and technical small print.
recipes.layout: stage plus apparatus, performer plus microphone, marquee hierarchy.
recipes.imagery: horn speakers, projection booth, singer, curtain, title card.
recipes.motion: silent card dissolves into voice, waveform pulse, curtain open.
recipes.risk: treating early sound as fully polished modern audio.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: mechanical awkwardness and theatrical novelty.

promptSeeds:
Design this through a 1927 lens: Futura has entered commercial type, the

examples.title: 1928: the machine becomes method

examples.feeling: modernism becomes a manual for use

CIAM turns modern architecture into an international program

machine travel, tubular furniture, and early aviation glamour sharpen the streamlined imagination

recipes.name: New Typography manual
recipes.useFor: documentation, civic services, editorial systems, forms, product onboarding.
recipes.palette: black, red, white, photographic grey, paper cream.
recipes.type: sans-serif, asymmetric hierarchy, bold rules, standardized sizes.
recipes.layout: function-first grid, active white space, clear entry points.
recipes.imagery: cropped photography, diagrams, arrows, factual captions.
recipes.motion: blocks sort, labels snap, photos crop into position.
recipes.risk: making it look like later Swiss Style instead of urgent 1928 reform.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: Tschichold's practical print concerns and asymmetry.

recipes.layout: site plan, dwelling unit, circulation diagram, sunlight logic.
recipes.imagery: terraces, windows, communal spaces, diagrams, models.
recipes.motion: plan unfolds into elevation, population flows, sunlight tracks.

recipes.name: Sound cartoon timing

recipes.layout: stage frame, repeated actions, musical bars, character silhouette.
recipes.imagery: steamboat, whistle, rubber-hose limbs, synchronized props.
recipes.motion: bounce, loop, beat hit, sound gag, squash and stretch.

examples.title: 1929: glass before the fall
examples.subtitle: The Barcelona Pavilion, E-1027, Film und Foto, MoMA, talking musicals, and the crash make the decade's polished modern surfaces feel suddenly fragile.
examples.feeling: polished modernity at the edge of collapse
primaryLens: the Barcelona Pavilion turns modern architecture into marble, glass, chrome, and space
Eileen Gray's E-1027 makes modern domesticity intimate, adjustable, and coastal
Film und Foto makes New Vision photography and film central to modern graphic culture
MoMA opens and begins institutionalizing modern art and design in New York
the Wall Street crash breaks the decade's luxury confidence and redirects modernism toward austerity

recipes.name: Barcelona ceremonial
recipes.useFor: architecture, luxury systems, museums, hospitality, high-culture brands.
recipes.palette: travertine cream, marble green, onyx gold, chrome, black water.
recipes.type: classical caps with restrained modern spacing.
recipes.layout: floating planes, axial calm, reflecting pool, sparse object placement.
recipes.imagery: glass, stone slabs, chrome X-frame, curtain, water, horizon.
recipes.motion: slow pan, reflection shift, plane sliding past plane.
recipes.risk: confusing Barcelona modernism with generic minimal luxury.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: Mies and Reich's temporary exposition ceremony and material specificity.

recipes.name: E-1027 adjustable
recipes.useFor: home products, furniture, wellness, travel, intimate interiors, hospitality.
recipes.palette: white, navy, canvas, chrome, warm wood, sea grey.
recipes.type: practical sans with nautical labels and small annotations.
recipes.layout: built-ins, pivoting tables, screens, bedside reach, view framing.
recipes.imagery: maps, deck railings, adjustable furniture, shutters, rugs, sea light.
recipes.motion: table swivels, screen folds, window opens, shadow moves.
recipes.risk: erasing Eileen Gray's authorship into generic Corbusian whiteness.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: user-specific adjustments and coastal domestic intimacy.

recipes.name: Film und Foto eye
recipes.useFor: editorial, photography tools, archives, cultural programs, camera brands.
recipes.palette: black, white, silver grey, red accent, paper cream.
recipes.type: New Typography sans, asymmetric captions, strong scale contrast.
recipes.layout: crop, sequence, diagonal, photomontage, exhibition label clarity.
recipes.imagery: camera, worker, city, eye, machine, high-angle and low-angle views.
recipes.motion: cut, crop, zoom, montage, contact sheet progression.
recipes.risk: making it look like generic photo zine modernism.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: New Vision angles, film sequence, and exhibition context.

recipes.name: Crash-era Deco
recipes.useFor: finance, theater, historical fiction, nightlife, luxury critique.
recipes.palette: black, brass, cream, deep green, oxblood, smoke grey.
recipes.type: Deco capitals with sober serif support.
recipes.layout: vertical ascent interrupted by diagonal fracture or falling ticker line.
recipes.imagery: skyscraper, stock ticker, theater marquee, champagne glass, newspaper.
recipes.motion: upward lines stall, ticker accelerates, light dims, paper falls.
recipes.risk: romanticizing the crash as glamorous moodboard drama.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: October 1929 economic rupture and the end of easy speculation.

examples.title: 1930: chrome at the cliff edge

examples.feeling: chrome optimism at the edge of scarcity

talkies, radio, and modern advertising compress image, sound, and brand

recipes.name: Chrysler crown
recipes.useFor: architectural brands, hotels, title sequences, luxury transport, civic identities.
recipes.palette: black, chrome, teal, warm cream, amber.
recipes.type: tall Deco capitals, narrow sans, centered names.

recipes.name: Depression streamline
recipes.useFor: tools, office products, appliances, productivity software, repair services.

recipes.layout: horizontal bands, rounded boxes, product-first composition.
recipes.imagery: smooth casings, simplified controls, clean desks, reliable mechanisms.
recipes.motion: cover closes, handle turns, paper feeds, object settles into place.
recipes.risk: making everything look 1940s-aerodynamic too early.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: transitional streamlining, not full rocket-age speed.

examples.title: 1948: instant, long-playing, reconstructed
examples.subtitle: Polaroid makes the photograph immediate, Columbia makes the record long-playing, the Marshall Plan begins rebuilding Europe, and London stages austerity under Olympic light. Modernity becomes a format change.
examples.feeling: the future becomes a new format
primaryLens: the polaroid model 95 turns photography into instant feedback
the columbia lp changes music packaging, duration, and listening
marshall plan reconstruction makes modern communication geopolitical
london's olympics show ceremony under austerity conditions
cobra and musique concrete push postwar art toward experiment

recipes.name: Instant photograph ritual
recipes.useFor: camera products, social apps, memory tools, onboarding, creative software.
recipes.palette: black leatherette, cream border, chemical sepia, warning red, metal grey.
recipes.type: instructional sans with numbered steps and small technical labels.
recipes.layout: sequence panels, waiting windows, peel-apart diagrams, object-centered framing.
recipes.imagery: hands pulling film, bordered prints, camera bellows, demonstration tables.
recipes.motion: expose, pull, wait, peel, reveal.
recipes.risk: using later white Polaroid nostalgia too early.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: Model 95 bulk, sepia-toned early prints, and process timing.

recipes.name: Long-playing sleeve
recipes.useFor: music, podcasts, archives, cultural publishing, collections.
recipes.palette: deep black, warm cream, burgundy, Columbia blue, label gold.
recipes.type: title hierarchy, side numbers, track lists, serif notes with modern display.
recipes.layout: square composition, central image, liner-note blocks, catalogue details.
recipes.imagery: record grooves, turntable, sleeve edges, orchestra or jazz photography.
recipes.motion: needle drop, slow rotation, side change, track progression.
recipes.risk: jumping to 1960s album-cover psychedelia.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: microgroove novelty and restrained early LP packaging.

recipes.name: Marshall Plan clarity
recipes.useFor: civic campaigns, dashboards, international programs, infrastructure.
recipes.palette: blue, red, cream, grey, muted green.
recipes.type: neutral sans, map labels, statistical captions.
recipes.layout: arrows, charts, production diagrams, national comparison tables.
recipes.imagery: factories, farms, ships, bridges, hands exchanging goods.
recipes.motion: route lines, bar growth, map fills, cargo flow.
recipes.risk: empty bureaucratic infographic style.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: recovery politics and cooperative economic messaging.

recipes.name: Austerity spectacle
recipes.useFor: events, sports, ceremonies, public broadcasts, cultural festivals.
recipes.palette: stadium grey, flag red, navy, white, grass green.
recipes.type: official sans, national identifiers, program typography.
recipes.layout: flags, lanes, schedules, seating diagrams, restrained ceremony.
recipes.imagery: Wembley, athletes, stopwatches, medals, broadcast cameras.
recipes.motion: procession, torch movement, scoreboard updates, camera pans.
recipes.risk: making it look like a lavish later Olympics.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: London 1948's rationing and postwar restraint.

recipes.name: Forty-five format

recipes.name: Festival modern

examples.title: 1953: systems begin to teach
examples.subtitle: Ulm is founded, the coronation is televised, fiberglass becomes automotive glamour, and domestic products start to behave like small coordinated systems.
examples.feeling: modern design starts explaining itself as a system
primaryLens: hfg ulm turns postwar modernism toward method, systems, and social responsibility
televised ceremony makes broadcast design a mass ritual
fiberglass leaves the chair and enters the sports car
home products become playful, modular, and branded
science fiction, magazines, and consumer imagery sharpen popular modern taste

recipes.name: Ulm system school
recipes.useFor: education, civic technology, design systems, product documentation, public information.
recipes.palette: off-white, black, warm grey, red-orange, muted blue.
recipes.type: rational sans-serif, strong hierarchy, diagrams, numbered modules.
recipes.layout: grid, workshops, process boards, exploded systems, research notes.
recipes.imagery: tools, models, pictograms, product parts, communication diagrams.
recipes.motion: sequence, comparison, assembly, information reveal.
recipes.risk: making Ulm look like generic Bauhaus nostalgia.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: postwar social purpose and system thinking.

recipes.name: Fiberglass speed
recipes.useFor: automotive brands, sports products, mobility, launch campaigns.
recipes.palette: white, red, chrome, asphalt black, signal yellow.
recipes.type: confident sans-serif with showroom display lettering.
recipes.layout: low car silhouette, diagonal road, specification panels, brochure spreads.
recipes.imagery: Corvette body, fiberglass curves, wheel detail, highway, showroom lights.
recipes.motion: reveal under cloth, road sweep, gauge movement, body highlight.
recipes.risk: drifting into late-1950s tailfin excess.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: early fiberglass novelty and Motorama-style display.

recipes.name: Television ceremony
recipes.useFor: live events, broadcast packages, public institutions, commemorations.
recipes.palette: black-and-white grey, royal red, gold, cream.
recipes.type: formal titles paired with broadcast captions.
recipes.layout: centered ceremony, screen frame, schedules, household viewing cues.
recipes.imagery: cameras, crowns, studio lights, living rooms, aerials, announcer desks.
recipes.motion: slow dissolve, title card, camera pan, signal fade.
recipes.risk: making it too high-definition or modern broadcast.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: small screens, limited contrast, and shared viewing ritual.

recipes.name: Convenience tray
recipes.useFor: food products, domestic apps, meal planning, packaging studies.
recipes.palette: foil silver, freezer blue, red, cream, peas green.
recipes.type: package display, instructions, heating times, compartment labels.
recipes.layout: tray grid, meal compartments, front-of-pack claims, domestic scene.
recipes.imagery: aluminum tray, frozen meal, television set, kitchen counter, printed box.
recipes.motion: freezer pull, package open, tray slide, screen glow.
recipes.risk: treating convenience as timeless rather than newly designed behavior.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: packaging plus ritual, not just food illustration.

examples.title: 1954: precision takes flight
examples.subtitle: Gullwing doors, Stratocaster curves, geodesic patents, Italian design prizes, brutalist schoolrooms, and Godzilla's radioactive shadow pull modern design toward speed, systems, and myth.
examples.feeling: precision dreaming under an atomic shadow
primaryLens: high-performance products make precision glamorous
italian industrial design gains an explicit prize culture
geodesic thinking turns structure into a repeatable spatial system
disneyland begins translating entertainment into planned environment
atomic trauma and monster cinema darken the space-age dream

recipes.name: Gullwing precision
recipes.useFor: automotive brands, performance tools, engineering launches, luxury hardware.
recipes.palette: silver, cream, black, racing red, teal shadow.
recipes.type: technical sans with brochure display type and specification tables.
recipes.layout: low angle, diagonal road, badge, specs, hinge detail, showroom space.
recipes.imagery: gullwing doors, tubular frame, chrome, leather, speed lines, road tests.
recipes.motion: doors rising, engine start, highlight sweep, tachometer climb.
recipes.risk: drifting into generic luxury car advertising.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: engineering detail as identity, not just glamour.

recipes.name: Stratocaster body
recipes.useFor: music products, creator tools, performance brands, audio interfaces.
recipes.palette: sunburst, cream, black, chrome, surf green, red.
recipes.type: lively display paired with technical labels and control markings.
recipes.layout: guitar silhouette, knob labels, cable path, stage angle, catalogue spread.
recipes.imagery: contoured body, pickups, tremolo arm, amplifier cloth, hands, stage lights.
recipes.motion: string vibration, knob turn, pick stroke, amplifier hum.
recipes.risk: using later psychedelic or arena-rock language.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: early electric-guitar product logic and modular hardware.

recipes.name: Geodesic future
recipes.useFor: architecture concepts, sustainability tools, spatial computing, education.
recipes.palette: night black, cream, steel grey, yellow, teal.
recipes.type: diagrammatic sans-serif, numbered nodes, structural captions.
recipes.layout: triangular grid, dome section, node detail, efficiency comparison.
recipes.imagery: struts, hubs, spheres, patents, models, sky, exhibition structures.
recipes.motion: triangle subdivision, dome assembly, node-to-node connection.
recipes.risk: turning it into 1970s commune nostalgia.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: patent-era structural optimism and engineering language.

recipes.name: Atomic monster city
recipes.useFor: film campaigns, climate warnings, risk dashboards, speculative fiction.
recipes.palette: smoke grey, black, warning orange, sea green, searchlight white.
recipes.type: heavy condensed titles with official warning captions.
recipes.layout: city silhouette, monster scale, diagonal beams, newspaper urgency.
recipes.imagery: Godzilla silhouette, smoke, water, power lines, sirens, ruined streets.
recipes.motion: slow rise, searchlight sweep, shock cut, smoke reveal.
recipes.risk: campy monster nostalgia without nuclear trauma.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: post-Hiroshima and post-testing anxiety, not playful kitsch.

promptSeeds:
Design this through a 1954 lens: the Mercedes 300 SL has turned engineering into a
gullwing icon, the Fender Stratocaster has made the electric guitar a contoured
industrial object, and Godzilla has turned atomic trauma into moving-image myth.

Give me three 1954-informed directions:
1. Gullwing precision
2. Stratocaster body
3. Geodesic future
For each, explain the historical lineage, typography, materials, motion, and what
to avoid.

Critique this environmental design as if it were proposed in 1954. Is it closer
to Disneyland planning, New Brutalist school honesty, geodesic structure, or a
later space-age cliche?

examples.title: 1955: modern pleasure opens to the public
examples.subtitle: Disneyland turns environment into coordinated experience, Citroen makes a family car look like science fiction, Ulm gives postwar rationalism a school, and Saul Bass teaches film titles to move like graphic design.
examples.feeling: the future becomes something you can enter
primaryLens: leisure becomes designed as a total system, from theme park to motel to television set
European industry searches for humane rationalism after the war
automotive and appliance surfaces turn aerodynamics into domestic desire
corporate graphics and film titles become sharper, simpler, and more kinetic
atomic optimism coexists with civil-defense anxiety and Cold War display

recipes.name: Disneyland system
recipes.useFor: onboarding, education, maps, museums, hospitality, family products.
recipes.palette: warm cream, turquoise, coral, asphalt black, ticket-booth red.
recipes.type: friendly sans and script accents, clear directional labels, themed display sparingly.
recipes.layout: map-like zones, thresholds, paths, badges, queue logic, small repeated signs.
recipes.imagery: gates, flags, vehicles, icons, attractions, illustrated wayfinding.
recipes.motion: reveal by lands, guided path, gentle parallax, ticket-stub transitions.
recipes.risk: generic cartoon nostalgia.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: circulation logic and total-environment thinking.

recipes.name: Ulm rational
recipes.useFor: civic tools, product systems, documentation, education, technical brands.
recipes.palette: off-white, black, cool grey, muted teal, signal orange.
recipes.type: clean sans, restrained hierarchy, numerals and diagrams handled precisely.
recipes.layout: modular grid, asymmetric balance, labels aligned to function.
recipes.imagery: diagrams, product photographs, arrows, measured spacing, prototypes.
recipes.motion: calm sequencing, grid snaps, rational reveals.
recipes.risk: looking like generic Swiss minimalism without social purpose.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: a visible system for use, not just empty space.

recipes.name: DS future-body
recipes.useFor: mobility, hardware, premium tools, industrial storytelling.
recipes.palette: deep charcoal, cream, chrome, hydraulic green, Paris-show red.
recipes.type: elegant sans with restrained technical labels.
recipes.layout: low horizontal sweep, asymmetrical product hero, detail callouts.
recipes.imagery: aerodynamic profile, suspension rise, steering wheel, road reflection.
recipes.motion: slow lift, glide, hydraulic softness, headlight-like reveal.
recipes.risk: reducing the DS to generic retro car styling.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: engineering behavior as the design drama.

recipes.name: Bass title fracture
recipes.useFor: film, music, mental-health campaigns, editorial identity, motion systems.
recipes.palette: black, cream, scarlet, smoky grey.
recipes.type: bold sans, cut-paper irregularity, high-contrast title cards.
recipes.layout: broken bars, hard crops, centered shocks, timed negative space.
recipes.imagery: abstract arm forms, paper cuts, shadows, jazz-club tension.
recipes.motion: abrupt entrances, syncopated cuts, nervous pauses.
recipes.risk: copying Saul Bass without understanding reduction.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: one strong symbol that can survive motion and print.

promptSeeds:
Design this through a 1955 lens: Disneyland has just opened as a total designed
environment, Citroen has introduced the DS, HfG Ulm is making design systematic,
and Saul Bass has turned film titles into kinetic graphic identity. Keep spectacle
and rationalism in productive tension.

Give me three 1955-informed directions:
1. Disneyland system
2. Ulm rational
3. DS future-body
For each, explain the historical lineage, typography, color, material, motion,
and what to avoid.

Critique this interface as if it appeared in 1955. Is it a themed public system,
a post-Bauhaus rational tool, a corporate graphic identity, or automotive futurism?
What evidence supports that lineage?

Atomic clip art scattered without Cold War anxiety

examples.title: 1956: the object becomes a system
examples.subtitle: The Eames Lounge Chair, Saarinen's pedestal furniture, Braun's SK4, Rand's IBM identity, and Britain's Independent Group all make the modern object cleaner, more domestic, and more aware of media culture.
examples.feeling: modernism becomes comfortable, branded, and media-aware
primaryLens: mid-century furniture refines comfort into sculptural industrial form
Braun and Ulm turn electronics into disciplined product systems
corporate identity begins replacing one-off advertising with repeatable marks
Pop thinking surfaces through exhibitions, mass media, and consumer imagery
highways, television, and rock and roll accelerate American everyday modernity

recipes.name: Lounge modern
recipes.useFor: premium interiors, editorial, audio products, reading apps, hospitality.

recipes.type: restrained sans with warm spacing and small elegant captions.

recipes.risk: generic luxury furniture photography.

recipes.name: Braun clarity
recipes.useFor: hardware, audio tools, settings screens, appliances, health devices.
recipes.palette: warm white, graphite, brushed metal, clear acrylic, muted red indicator.
recipes.type: precise sans, small labels, rational control hierarchy.
recipes.layout: front-panel logic, aligned controls, visible affordances, measured modules.
recipes.imagery: knobs, grids, transparent lids, speakers, manuals, product diagrams.
recipes.motion: lid open, dial turn, signal light, orderly state change.
recipes.risk: confusing 1956 Braun with later Apple minimalism.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: tactile controls and analog use.

recipes.name: Corporate mark system

recipes.type: strong sans or slab-inflected corporate lettering, consistent lockups.
recipes.layout: identity standards, repeatable placements, stationery grids, machine labels.
recipes.imagery: offices, machines, punched cards, signage, instruction sheets.
recipes.motion: mark assembly, stamp, repeat, align, file.
recipes.risk: making the logo a one-off poster trick.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: standards logic across many applications.

recipes.name: Proto-Pop collage

recipes.motion: cut-and-paste jumps, zooms, channel changes, paper slide.

recipes.addAccuracyWith: Independent Group media curiosity and postwar British context.

Design this through a 1956 lens: the Eames Lounge Chair, Saarinen's pedestal

sectionMap.description: 1966 moving image is pop, spy, mod, and increasingly fragmented.

sectionMap.label: Surface
sectionMap.description: 1966 color is moving from optical contrast into psychedelic saturation.

sectionMap.label: Anti-cliches
sectionMap.description: Do not make 1966 look like:

examples.title: 1967: total color world
examples.subtitle: Expo 67, Sgt. Pepper, the Summer of Love, and psychedelic poster culture turn design into an environment: graphic systems, album worlds, counterculture, and modular futures all bloom at once.
examples.feeling: the world as a designed happening
primaryLens: psychedelic design reaches a public high point through music and posters
expo 67 proves that global modernism can be modular, branded, and spectacular
album packaging becomes an immersive cultural artifact
counterculture turns clothing, print, interiors, and events into total identity
pop, op, swiss, and radical architecture coexist rather than replacing one another

recipes.name: Summer of Love poster
recipes.useFor: music festivals, cultural events, community campaigns, experiential brands.
recipes.palette: magenta, orange, violet, acid green, turquoise, warm yellow.
recipes.type: hand-lettered, swollen, curved, dense, integrated with image.
recipes.layout: all-over field, ornamental border, central face or figure, little empty space.
recipes.imagery: hair, flowers, eyes, instruments, Victorian curves, cosmic symbols.
recipes.motion: liquid morph, slow color cycle, projected light, breathing pattern.
recipes.risk: lazy rainbow hippie cliche.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: specific San Francisco venue-poster craft and print texture.

recipes.name: Expo modular optimism
recipes.useFor: civic systems, wayfinding, museums, public technology, education platforms.
recipes.palette: white, concrete grey, signal red, blue, green, black.
recipes.type: clear sans, pictograms, multilingual hierarchy, map labels.
recipes.layout: modular grid, visitor paths, pavilion modules, repeated units.
recipes.imagery: domes, modules, maps, flags, crowds, transit, terraces.
recipes.motion: route tracing, module stacking, pavilion reveal, map zoom.
recipes.risk: generic midcentury optimism without crowd-scale function.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: wayfinding and architecture working as one system.

recipes.name: Sgt. Pepper collage stage
recipes.useFor: album campaigns, cultural retrospectives, theater, identity launches.
recipes.palette: saturated red, blue, yellow, pink, military gold, grass green.
recipes.type: decorative display mixed with clean credits and object labels.
recipes.layout: frontal tableau, dense cast, symmetrical stage, collectible details.
recipes.imagery: celebrity cutouts, uniforms, flowers, instruments, props, hand-built sets.
recipes.motion: curtain reveal, character pan, collage assembly, camera push-in.
recipes.risk: copying the cover without understanding collage-as-mythology.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: theatrical staging and named cultural references.

recipes.name: Counterculture room
recipes.useFor: social spaces, community products, independent publishing, wellness-adjacent brands.
recipes.palette: warm earth, violet, saffron, deep red, faded denim blue.
recipes.type: hand lettering, underground press headlines, imperfect alignment.
recipes.layout: posters on walls, floor seating, layered textiles, record-centered zones.
recipes.imagery: cushions, incense, plants, records, handmade clothing, light projections.
recipes.motion: lamp glow, film projection, smoke drift, rotating record.
recipes.risk: turning 1967 into generic boho lifestyle.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: political print, music objects, and handmade imperfection.

promptSeeds:
Design this through a 1967 lens: Expo 67 has made global modernism into a
walkable system, the Summer of Love has made posters and clothing into
counterculture identity, and Sgt. Pepper has turned the album cover into a
mythic collage stage. Keep these strands distinct.

Give me three 1967-informed directions:
1. Summer of Love poster
2. Expo modular optimism
3. Sgt. Pepper collage stage
For each, explain historical lineage, typography, palette, environment, motion,
and what to avoid.

Critique this identity as if it launched in 1967. Is it Expo wayfinding, San
Francisco psychedelia, Push Pin historicism, or album-world collage? What visual
evidence proves the lineage?

objects: Habitat 67 modular housing units as architectural-object reference
Buckminster Fuller's geodesic dome at Expo 67
Records, inserts, and posters connected to Sgt. Pepper
Mod and counterculture clothing: military jackets, velvet, beads, boots, denim
Portable record players, light-show equipment, and poster-covered room objects
printAndGraphics: Peter Blake and Jann Haworth's Sgt. Pepper cover
Milton Glaser's Bob Dylan poster
Expo 67 identity, maps, pictograms, and signage by Georges Huel and collaborators
San Francisco rock posters for the Fillmore and Avalon
First issues and early layouts of Rolling Stone
spaces: Expo 67 in Montreal, including Habitat 67 and the U.S. geodesic dome
Haight-Ashbury during the Summer of Love
Monterey International Pop Festival
The Fillmore Auditorium and Avalon Ballroom
Communal apartments, poster shops, underground newspaper offices, and light-show venues

antiCliches: Tie-dye without poster, music, or print context
A generic peace-sign collage from the entire late sixties
Woodstock imagery, which belongs to 1969
Disco, lava lamps, and 1970s dorm-room nostalgia
Psychedelic illegibility with no hand-lettering craft
Expo modernism reduced to bland world-fair optimism
Sgt. Pepper reduced to random celebrity cutouts
Counterculture style with no politics, publishing, or community

examples.title: 1968: tools for outer and inner space
examples.subtitle: Kubrick's white spacecraft, the Whole Earth Catalog, student revolt, Yellow Submarine, and radical architecture make design feel like a toolkit for changing perception, technology, and society.
examples.feeling: cosmic precision under political pressure
primaryLens: 2001 makes space-age modernism precise, silent, and cinematic
the whole earth catalog links counterculture, tools, ecology, and information design
yellow submarine translates pop psychedelia into animated graphic worlds
protest movements turn posters, walls, and streets into urgent media
radical design shifts from object style toward systems, environments, and participation

recipes.name: Kubrick space system
recipes.useFor: AI products, aerospace, premium interfaces, speculative film, high-trust tools.
recipes.palette: white, black, red, cool grey, screen blue.
recipes.type: clean sans, sparse labels, airline-like hierarchy, calm warnings.
recipes.layout: circular paths, centered panels, white space, instrument logic.
recipes.imagery: spacecraft interiors, red chairs, helmets, screens, Earth and stars.
recipes.motion: slow rotation, silent glide, screen blink, mechanical door movement.
recipes.risk: generic sci-fi chrome.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: 1968 restraint, practical sets, and corporate-aerospace calm.

recipes.name: Whole Earth interface
recipes.useFor: knowledge bases, maker tools, climate products, community platforms.
recipes.palette: black ink, paper white, earth brown, utility red, blueprint blue.
recipes.type: pragmatic serif and sans mixes, captions, prices, addresses, annotations.
recipes.layout: dense catalog entries, reviews, diagrams, cross-references, margins.
recipes.imagery: tools, domes, books, maps, farms, machines, hands.
recipes.motion: page flip, index jump, zoom into diagram, network link.
recipes.risk: making it too polished or too tech-startup clean.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: tool usefulness, source notes, and information density.

recipes.name: Atelier Populaire urgency
recipes.useFor: activism, civic campaigns, social movements, emergency communications.
recipes.palette: black, red, rough white, single-color blue or brown.
recipes.type: stencil, hand-cut block letters, direct slogans, minimal hierarchy.
recipes.layout: one image, one slogan, poster-first, fast reproduction.
recipes.imagery: fists, factories, silhouettes, police, students, flags, streets.
recipes.motion: paste-up, screenprint pull, wall accumulation, marching rhythm.
recipes.risk: aestheticizing protest without a real message.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: collective production and specific political context.

recipes.name: Yellow Submarine animation
recipes.useFor: children's media, music visuals, playful education, surreal brands.
recipes.palette: lemon yellow, cobalt, pink, orange, teal, black.
recipes.type: playful rounded display, hand lettering, comic labels.
recipes.layout: flat worlds, character paths, pattern zones, impossible landscapes.
recipes.imagery: submarines, monsters, flowers, marching figures, music notes.
recipes.motion: cutout movement, morphing shapes, looped walks, musical transitions.
recipes.risk: generic psychedelic clip art.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: Heinz Edelmann-style graphic wit and animated flatness.

promptSeeds:
Design this through a 1968 lens: 2001 has made space-age modernism calm and

1969 Woodstock nostalgia
Generic NASA fan art with later shuttle-era details
Clean sci-fi UI with contemporary glassmorphism

sectionMap.description: 1968 is pulled between controlled futurism and improvised resistance.

sectionMap.description: 2001: A Space Odyssey is released, The first Whole Earth Catalog appears, Yellow Submarine...

sectionMap.description: 1968 typography ranges from coldly perfect to urgently rough.

sectionMap.label: Graphic design

sectionMap.label: Product design

examples.title: 1969: moonshot afterimage
examples.subtitle: Apollo 11, Woodstock, Concorde, Easy Rider, and late psychedelic culture make 1969 feel like a triumph, a hangover, and a launch platform for the seventies all at once.
examples.feeling: a future achieved just as the dream changes shape
primaryLens: apollo 11 turns the moon landing into the century's defining media image
woodstock makes counterculture visible as crowd, poster, mud, and myth
concorde and aerospace design keep technological elegance alive
late psychedelia darkens into earthier, heavier, more political forms
design begins crossing from sixties optimism into seventies ecology and systems

recipes.name: Apollo evidence
recipes.useFor: science platforms, mission-driven teams, archives, aerospace, high-trust interfaces.
recipes.palette: lunar grey, suit white, black sky, gold visor, NASA blue, warning red.
recipes.type: technical sans, mission labels, checklists, telemetry, small captions.
recipes.layout: procedure panels, image evidence, numbered steps, patch plus diagram.
recipes.imagery: lunar module, boot prints, helmets, Earth, control rooms, scan lines.
recipes.motion: slow transmission, signal lock, checklist advance, lunar pan.
recipes.risk: using later space-shuttle or generic sci-fi imagery.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: Apollo 11 hardware, broadcast texture, and procedural detail.

recipes.name: Woodstock field myth
recipes.useFor: festivals, community gatherings, music campaigns, outdoor culture.
recipes.palette: grass green, mud brown, denim blue, poster red, warm yellow, cloudy white.
recipes.type: simple hand lettering, folk-rock display, poster headline, ticket text.
recipes.layout: emblem plus field, crowd scale, stage axis, documentary photo grid.
recipes.imagery: dove and guitar, blankets, sound towers, rain, handmade clothes, crowds.
recipes.motion: film grain, crowd wave, stage pan, rain and mud texture.
recipes.risk: generic peace-sign nostalgia.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: Arnold Skolnick's symbolic simplicity and real festival infrastructure.

recipes.name: Concorde silhouette
recipes.useFor: mobility, aviation, premium travel, speed, engineering brands.
recipes.palette: white, sky blue, runway black, metal grey, signal red.
recipes.type: sleek sans, aviation numerals, route maps, technical labels.
recipes.layout: long horizontal silhouette, delta geometry, route line, nose profile.
recipes.imagery: delta wing, pointed nose, runway, clouds, control tower, boarding stairs.
recipes.motion: taxi, nose angle, takeoff acceleration, route trace.
recipes.risk: generic jet-age luxury from the 1950s.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: 1969 first-flight context and supersonic engineering form.

recipes.name: Road-end counterculture
recipes.useFor: film campaigns, apparel, documentary brands, music editorials.
recipes.palette: faded denim, leather black, flag red, dust tan, chrome, sunset orange.
recipes.type: heavy condensed display, hand-painted road signs, sparse credits.
recipes.layout: road horizon, motorcycle profile, wide landscape, poster still.
recipes.imagery: helmets, flags, gas stations, desert, jackets, sunglasses.
recipes.motion: highway tracking, engine vibration, film-grain dissolve, sunset cut.
recipes.risk: biker cliche without 1969 ambivalence.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: Easy Rider unease, not just freedom fantasy.

promptSeeds:
Design this through a 1969 lens: Apollo 11 has made the Moon landing a live

Concorde has made supersonic travel a sleek silhouette. Keep moonshot evidence,

Communes, crash pads, poster-covered music rooms, and tool-catalog workspaces

antiCliches: A generic collage of all sixties symbols
Woodstock without mud, scale, logistics, or documentary texture
Apollo imagery mixed with shuttle-era interfaces
Psychedelic posters from 1966 treated as if nothing changed
Clean utopian futurism with no political fatigue
1970s disco or shag-carpet stereotypes
Concorde as 1950s jet-age clip art

examples.title: 1970: ecology meets the megastructure
examples.subtitle: The decade opens with Expo optimism, environmental alarm, corporate systems, plastic curves, and a new suspicion that progress has a bill attached.
examples.feeling: progress turning earthy, systematic, and nervous
primaryLens: expo 70 makes technology spectacular and environmental anxiety public
corporate identity systems turn modernism into everyday infrastructure
earth day gives ecology a mass graphic and political vocabulary
plastic furniture and boutique fashion soften the machine with color and body
research labs begin quietly sketching the computer interface future

recipes.name: Expo ecology

recipes.name: Plastic lounge future

recipes.name: Signal mark

examples.title: 1972: systems start to misbehave

recipes.name: Civic pictogram system
recipes.useFor: wayfinding, public services, events, healthcare, transportation, civic tech.
recipes.palette: white, sky blue, grass green, silver grey, black.
recipes.type: light modern sans, pictograms as equal partners.
recipes.layout: strict grid, generous spacing, modular sign families.
recipes.imagery: simplified bodies, arrows, sport/action symbols, maps.
recipes.motion: clean transitions, icon assembly, route highlighting.
recipes.risk: generic airport icons with no humane rhythm.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: Aicher-like body geometry and consistent stroke logic.

recipes.name: Controversial diagram
recipes.useFor: maps, data products, transit tools, dashboards, information design.
recipes.palette: black, white, route red, blue, green, yellow.
recipes.type: Helvetica-like labels, station dots, strict alignment.
recipes.layout: 45/90-degree logic, simplified geography, color-coded routes.
recipes.imagery: lines, nodes, transfers, rivers as graphic boundaries.
recipes.motion: route trace, transfer snap, diagram-to-street reveal.
recipes.risk: confusing visual clarity with user truth.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: visible tradeoffs between diagram and geography.

recipes.name: Instant image ritual

recipes.palette: warm white, black, tan leather, rainbow stripe, chemical grey.
recipes.type: product labels, film-pack instructions, restrained sans.
recipes.layout: object closed/open, square image frame, instruction sequence.
recipes.imagery: folding camera, developing print, hand-held image, table light.
recipes.motion: unfold, shoot, eject, wait, image appear.

recipes.name: Screen play primitive

promptSeeds:
Design this through a 1976 lens: the United States Bicentennial has turned
history into coordinated public graphics, Apple has just been founded, VHS has
entered the format war, Concorde is in service, and punk is beginning to rip the
page. Keep official spectacle and anti-design urgency both visible.

Give me three 1976-informed directions:
1. Bicentennial system
2. Punk ignition
3. Format war living room
For each, explain typography, material, palette, motion, and the specific
historical mistake to avoid.

Critique this poster as if it were made in 1976. Is it commemorative public
graphics, punk collage, disco travel glamour, arcade minimalism, or home-video
product design? What evidence supports the answer?

objects: Apple I computer board
Atari Breakout arcade cabinet and screen graphics
JVC VHS video recorder and cassette
Sony Betamax machines in the early format war
Concorde aircraft and passenger-service materials
Bicentennial commemorative coins, posters, packaging, and public objects
Punk clothing from Malcolm McLaren and Vivienne Westwood's London scene
printAndGraphics: United States Bicentennial identity applications and commemorative graphics
Sex Pistols "Anarchy in the U.K." promotional graphics
Ramones debut album sleeve
Punk flyers, zines, and boutique graphics from London and New York
Taxi Driver, Network, and Logan's Run posters and title atmospheres
Arcade cabinet marquees and instruction cards
spaces: Bicentennial public events, exhibitions, and parade environments
London punk spaces around King's Road and early club venues
New York clubs including CBGB
Domestic living rooms with video machines and hi-fi equipment
Concorde cabins and airport lounges
The Centre Pompidou construction site as high-tech architecture becoming visible

antiCliches: Fully formed 1977 punk without the bicentennial and disco counterweight
Random ransom-note type with no cheap print logic
Flag graphics without public-program structure
Apple design as if the Macintosh already exists
VHS culture as if video stores dominate yet
Concorde as generic futurism without elite travel and aerodynamic form
Disco as only mirror balls, with no urban anxiety or fashion system
Corporate modernism as untouched by anger

examples.title: 1977: pop futures and torn signals
examples.subtitle: Star Wars, Studio 54, the Apple II, Atari VCS, the Centre Pompidou, the I Love New York logo, and punk graphics make the year feel like mass culture splitting into icons.
examples.feeling: icons arriving from opposite directions
primaryLens: star wars turns cinema into a total design and merchandising universe
punk graphics become internationally legible through jamie reid and the sex pistols
i love new york proves a tiny mark can carry civic emotion at enormous scale
apple ii and atari vcs bring approachable computing and games into homes
studio 54 and disco turn nightlife into theatrical identity

recipes.name: Civic heart mark
recipes.useFor: city brands, tourism, public campaigns, cultural initiatives, merchandise systems.
recipes.palette: black, white, red, municipal blue, warm paper.
recipes.type: typewriter slab or friendly geometric, simple stacking, symbol as word.
recipes.layout: compact lockup, generous space, repeatable souvenir scale.
recipes.imagery: heart symbol, city name, map fragments, buttons, posters, tote bags.
recipes.motion: stamp, blink, street-poster repeat, souvenir reveal.
recipes.risk: making it cute without civic urgency.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: emotional compression and brutal simplicity.

recipes.name: Punk tear sheet
recipes.useFor: music launches, zines, protest graphics, fashion capsules, youth campaigns.
recipes.palette: hot pink, acid yellow, black, white, dirty newsprint.
recipes.type: ransom fragments, crude sans, typewriter captions, pasted label blocks.
recipes.layout: torn rectangles, crooked overlays, aggressive scale, cheap reproduction.
recipes.imagery: defaced portraits, monarchy fragments, newspaper scraps, safety pins.
recipes.motion: rip, paste, photocopy flash, abrupt jump cut.
recipes.risk: polished grunge cosplay.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: specific Jamie Reid-era provocation and print scarcity.

recipes.name: Used-universe pop myth
recipes.useFor: entertainment brands, games, toys, sci-fi products, story worlds.
recipes.palette: desert tan, black space, laser red, droid blue, worn off-white.
recipes.type: cinematic logo, crawl-like perspective, technical labels for objects.
recipes.layout: heroic central emblem, toy-card hierarchy, poster painting drama.
recipes.imagery: battered ships, helmets, droids, stars, old machinery, desert planets.
recipes.motion: opening crawl, hyperspace streak, model pass, wipe transition.
recipes.risk: clean generic sci-fi chrome.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: old, repaired, merchandisable world-building.

recipes.name: Living-room cartridge
recipes.useFor: game platforms, playful tools, education products, hardware interfaces.
recipes.palette: woodgrain brown, black, orange, cream, TV blue.
recipes.type: simple sans labels, cartridge titles, score numerals, manual diagrams.
recipes.layout: console front, cartridge grid, TV frame, controller cord paths.
recipes.imagery: Atari VCS, joysticks, cartridges, family room carpet, Apple II desk.
recipes.motion: cartridge insert, power switch, scanline flicker, blocky score change.
recipes.risk: confusing 1977 with later 8-bit nostalgia.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: woodgrain console tactility and early home-computer awkwardness.

promptSeeds:
Design this through a 1977 lens: Star Wars has made science fiction a total
merchandising universe, I LOVE NY has compressed civic emotion into a tiny mark,
Studio 54 has made nightlife theatrical, and punk graphics are tearing the page.
Keep each icon system historically distinct.

Give me three 1977-informed directions:
1. Civic heart mark
2. Punk tear sheet
3. Used-universe pop myth
For each, explain typography, palette, product logic, motion, and the main
anachronism to avoid.

Critique this interface as if it launched in 1977. Is it closer to Apple II,
Atari VCS, Star Wars merchandising, Centre Pompidou high-tech, Studio 54 disco,
or punk print culture? What evidence supports that reading?

spaces: Centre Pompidou in Paris

antiCliches: Only punk, with no disco, computing, civic identity, or Star Wars
Smooth digital interfaces that belong after the Macintosh
Generic sci-fi chrome instead of used-universe repair and toy logic
Random heart icons without Glaser's type-and-symbol compression
Studio 54 reduced to mirror balls without door, fashion, and social theater
Atari as late-1980s arcade nostalgia rather than living-room woodgrain
Centre Pompidou as generic industrial chic without public infrastructure logic
Punk distress that looks like a Photoshop filter

examples.title: 1978: corporate clean under arcade pressure
examples.subtitle: Space Invaders, Superman, Grease, the National Gallery East Building, Delirious New York, post-punk sound, and late-modern polish make the year feel clean, crowded, and increasingly electronic.
examples.feeling: clean systems hearing electronic footsteps
primaryLens: space invaders turns the screen into a repeating graphic battlefield
corporate and cultural architecture sharpens through pei, high-tech, and postmodern debate
post-punk and new wave begin refining punk damage into style systems
film and music revive the past as designed mass entertainment
retail, fashion, and product surfaces move toward cleaner branded confidence

recipes.name: Arcade descent
recipes.useFor: games, alerts, dashboards, learning tools, countdown interfaces.
recipes.palette: black, phosphor green, white, red accent, cabinet orange.
recipes.type: blocky screen labels, score numerals, simple instruction text.
recipes.layout: rows, shields, player base, score bar, descending grid.
recipes.imagery: alien icons, cabinet glass, coin slots, instruction cards, CRT glow.
recipes.motion: stepwise descent, lateral march, sudden drop, score flash.
recipes.risk: using later 8-bit nostalgia instead of early arcade austerity.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: repetition, pressure, and simple icon behavior.

recipes.name: Post-punk clean
recipes.useFor: music, editorial, fashion, cultural brands, gallery campaigns.
recipes.palette: black, white, red, cold grey, muted skin tone.
recipes.type: stark sans, narrow spacing, restrained scale, mechanical alignment.
recipes.layout: empty space, single photograph, bands of color, severe crop.
recipes.imagery: uniforms, stripes, blank expressions, industrial props, night streets.
recipes.motion: metronomic cuts, freeze pose, light flicker, abrupt silence.
recipes.risk: making it too polished and losing unease.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: controlled discomfort rather than decorative grunge.

recipes.name: Museum geometry
recipes.useFor: cultural institutions, architecture studios, archives, civic platforms.
recipes.palette: limestone cream, charcoal, glass blue, warm grey, bronze.
recipes.type: clean modern sans, catalog hierarchy, measured captions.
recipes.layout: triangles, atrium axes, geometric plans, generous margins.
recipes.imagery: Pei-like angular forms, galleries, skylights, plans, stone surfaces.
recipes.motion: slow pan, triangular wipe, light crossing an atrium.
recipes.risk: generic museum minimalism.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: monumental geometry and institutional confidence.

recipes.name: Revival spectacle
recipes.useFor: entertainment campaigns, musicals, fashion events, pop products.
recipes.palette: bubblegum pink, leather black, cream, cherry red, stage blue.
recipes.type: script accents with bold film-title hierarchy.
recipes.layout: character group, dance floor, marquee, soundtrack modules.
recipes.imagery: jackets, school dance, cars, chorus lines, comic-book emblems.
recipes.motion: choreographed reveal, title glow, spin, crowd clap.
recipes.risk: mistaking 1950s source material for 1978 revival styling.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: visible seventies production gloss over the retro content.

examples.title: 1979: the portable future arrives
examples.subtitle: The Walkman privatizes sound, VisiCalc makes software feel useful, Alien turns biology into industrial horror, Unknown Pleasures makes data sublime, and the seventies end as a set of portable signals.
examples.feeling: private signals moving through public space
primaryLens: the sony walkman makes personal audio a product, fashion, and urban behavior
visicalc proves software can transform ordinary work on a personal computer
alien and apocalypse now make atmosphere, production design, and sound physically overwhelming
post-punk graphics refine data, austerity, and monochrome emotion
arcade, new wave, and video culture prepare the visual vocabulary of the eighties

recipes.name: Portable private audio

recipes.name: Data-black post-punk

recipes.type: small sans, restrained spacing, minimal caption logic.
recipes.layout: central data image, large negative space, quiet hierarchy.
recipes.imagery: pulsar plots, scientific traces, industrial landscapes, blank portraits.
recipes.motion: line trace, slow pulse, fade from black, signal drift.
recipes.risk: generic dark minimalism.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: real information treated as atmosphere, not random waveforms.

recipes.name: Biomechanical horror

recipes.risk: using later glossy monster sci-fi.

promptSeeds:

CD ubiquity as if it has already replaced all analog music
Modern social media charity aesthetics for Live Aid

examples.title: 1986: the desktop becomes a studio
examples.subtitle: Graphic design discovers that the Macintosh is not just a typesetting shortcut but a new surface for image, body, type, and error. The late-eighties future begins as a paste-up table with a screen glow.
examples.feeling: the paste-up table starts glowing
primaryLens: desktop publishing moves from novelty toward working method
april greiman makes the digital page bodily, photographic, and spatial
late memphis color and postmodern quotation keep disturbing corporate polish
broadcast graphics, music video, and youth media accelerate visual literacy
high-tech architecture and consumer electronics expose the system as style

recipes.name: Screen-body collage
recipes.useFor: design archives, experimental editorial, personal data tools, cultural essays.
recipes.palette: cream paper, black ink, hot pink, cyan, acid green, violet.
recipes.type: bitmap display mixed with geometric sans and small technical annotations.
recipes.layout: one long field, body-scale image, diagrams, overlays, measured fragments.
recipes.imagery: scanned body, halftone portraits, cosmic diagrams, screen captures, paste-up marks.
recipes.motion: vertical unfold, scan-line reveal, image registration drift.
recipes.risk: turning Greiman into random collage.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: a clear relationship between body, measurement, and digital mediation.

recipes.name: Late Memphis broadcast
recipes.useFor: youth brands, event identities, music packaging, retail graphics.
recipes.palette: black, white, hot pink, turquoise, yellow, violet.
recipes.type: fat display type, rotated captions, playful geometric lettering.
recipes.layout: asymmetry, diagonal bars, floating shapes, pattern blocks, loud margins.
recipes.imagery: squiggles, dots, laminate textures, video stills, product silhouettes.
recipes.motion: jump cuts, wipes, color-bar flashes, bouncing geometric accents.
recipes.risk: generic party Memphis with no late-eighties media edge.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: broadcast timing and printed halftone texture.

recipes.name: High-tech service diagram
recipes.useFor: infrastructure brands, architecture portfolios, engineering tools, civic technology.
recipes.palette: steel grey, black, safety yellow, red, cool blue.
recipes.type: condensed technical sans, labels, arrows, numbered parts.
recipes.layout: exposed systems, sectional logic, exteriorized components, grid annotation.
recipes.imagery: ducts, lifts, vents, pipes, cables, machine rooms, reflective metal.
recipes.motion: parts sliding outward, lift movement, schematic assembly.
recipes.risk: clean sci-fi minimalism.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: real service logic and visible maintenance access.

recipes.name: Domestic interface ritual
recipes.useFor: games, learning products, retro computing, family media tools.
recipes.palette: beige plastic, warm grey, black, red buttons, TV blue.
recipes.type: monospaced prompts, cartridge labels, manual typography.
recipes.layout: hardware frame, screen window, controller modules, instruction panels.
recipes.imagery: cartridges, floppies, cables, CRT glow, manuals, living-room furniture.
recipes.motion: boot, insert, blink, select, load, start.

promptSeeds:
Design this through a 1986 lens: April Greiman has just made the Macintosh page
bodily, cosmic, and unresolved, while desktop publishing is moving from novelty
to studio method. Keep the digital roughness visible instead of making it clean.

Give me three 1986-informed directions:
1. Screen-body collage
2. Late Memphis broadcast
3. High-tech service diagram
For each, explain the historical lineage, typography, color, material, motion,
and what to avoid.

Critique this layout as if it appeared in 1986. Does it understand desktop
publishing as a hybrid of paste-up, bitmap type, halftone image, and screen
composition, or is it using later digital polish?

antiCliches: Smooth 1990s Photoshop collage
Generic vaporwave pink grids
Perfectly clean Apple minimalism
Random Memphis shapes without print, retail, or object logic
Cyberpunk rain from 1982 repeated as a default future
Windows 95 desktop nostalgia
Digital type that is too high-resolution for the moment
Neon chrome with no paper, halftone, or service-bureau reality

recipes.name: Color vector studio

recipes.risk: making it look like later Illustrator or flat-design nostalgia.

recipes.name: HyperCard stack

examples.title: 1989: the network appears

examples.title: 1992: deconstruction goes public
examples.subtitle: Ray Gun launches, Windows 3.1 normalizes the GUI, the Barcelona Olympics stage playful civic modernity, and the page becomes a site where type, image, and sound can be deliberately unsettled.
examples.feeling: order is still present, but it no longer gets the final word
primaryLens: experimental editorial typography reaches a wider audience through Ray Gun
Windows 3.1 and the ThinkPad make graphical computing more ordinary and mobile
Barcelona uses architecture, pictograms, and urban repair as global image
MTV, alternative rock, rave, and hip-hop make graphic identity more fragmented
deconstruction becomes a practical taste rather than only a theoretical argument

recipes.layout: tables, left nav, simple banners, boxed controls, visible page logic.
recipes.imagery: tiny icons, dithered photos, badges, low-bandwidth illustrations.
recipes.motion: page load, cursor point, GIF loop, form submit.
recipes.risk: jumping ahead to social media or glossy Web 2.0.

recipes.name: Polygonal living room
recipes.useFor: games, youth electronics, entertainment launches, retro tech products.

recipes.layout: menu grids, character select panels, low-poly framing, logo-forward packaging.
recipes.imagery: polygons, controllers, CRT scan, discs, 3D mascots, rendered shadows.
recipes.motion: spin, load screen, camera orbit, polygon pop-in.

recipes.name: Synthetic character

promptSeeds:
Design this through a 1995 lens: Ray Gun and David Carson have made damaged
editorial type feel truthful, Windows 95 has made the GUI a mass ritual, and
Netscape has turned the browser into a cultural object. Keep print fracture and
early web constraints distinct.

Give me three 1995-informed directions:
1. Grunge editorial fracture
2. Browser dawn
3. Polygonal living room
For each, explain typography, material, interface logic, motion, and what to avoid.

Critique this layout as if it appeared in 1995. Is it Carson-influenced print,
early commercial web, PlayStation-era polygon culture, or Toy Story-like synthetic
character design? What evidence supports the lineage?

objects: Microsoft Windows 95 retail box and Start menu interface

Pixar's toy models and rendered plastic surfaces in Toy Story

PlayStation launch advertising and game packaging
spaces: Dorm rooms with CRTs, stereos, consoles, posters, and CD towers

Animation production spaces at Pixar
Electronics retail aisles displaying boxed software and consoles

antiCliches: A polished Web 2.0 startup
Generic hacker green code rain
Vaporwave sunsets and chrome palms
Clean Swiss minimalism with one distressed font pasted on top
Late-2000s social media nostalgia
Smooth photoreal CGI
Nu-metal graphics that belong later in the decade
A Windows XP desktop

examples.title: 1997: deconstruction becomes infrastructure
